dialog.content: I have anxiety, which keeps me awake at night and I can't relax

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: lots of people suffer from anxiety.you are not alone.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I just want to not having to live with it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: ok. well do you want to expand on what's causing the anxiety?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: A lot of reasons, but right now because my parents are old. I'm worried whenever they have to drive, especially in the snow

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sure your parents know that if they can't drive they won't because they you are worried about them

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: They still have to go to work. The point here is, that is just the tip of an iceberg. Most of the time, I am worried something bad will happen, even if there is no particular reason

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: have you spoken to them about your concern?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No I have not, I don't think they can help

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: well it's a atart. I suggest you talk to them about it. maybe they will take your concerns seriously

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Are there anything else that can help?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: well I suggest try exercising always keeps mind occupied

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: have you seek professional help?

dialog.content: No I have not, they can be very expensive

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
well they do a good job in helping others
 maybe it's worth a try

dialog.speaker: supporter

situation: Friends that I used to trust and they broke up the trust.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi I'm sean how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm not doing well, I was angry with my friend who broke up our trust And you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: what is a frie your boyfriend and how did he break up your trust?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, it's not my boyfriend, it's a friend

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So tell me what happened.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I used to trust my friend but she told everyone what I told her, however I told her that it's not to tell anyone.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Unfortunately, i hate to sterotype but women do this quite often.  it depends on the severity of the information you told her.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes, I know. I have no idea if i'm going to forgave her. Probably no
 Do you think I should forgive her?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If it was something extremley important, say jail worthy, I would get rid of her immediately.  If it was something dramatic but not extremely dangerous, I would sit down and haev a serious talk with her. It all depends on the situation, how long have you known her, what is your trust in her, how close are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It was my feelings. I know her for a long time. I can't trust her right now however I think I need time to think about it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ok let me think one second.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: ok

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Good friends are very hard to come by and find.   i would suggest sitting down with her and explaining how serious this hurt you and that she cannot let this happen again.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, it's true. But I'll wait one week and I'll talk to her

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would ultimately give an ultimate.  You will forgive her this one time.  If it happens again, anything even remotely similar, you part ways and say goodbye as you cannot trust her with important information.  If you give her important information and she tells everyone that's not a true friend I would advise to approach the situation as soon as possible to advocate the seriousness of the situation.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, i'll do it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Unless you don't think it's a big deal..... ;)  I know you can handle this well.  I wish you the best of luck darling.  I'm sure it will go much easier than expected but look her straight in the eyes and tell her what you want to say.  do not move eye contact

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
But I'm not going to talk to her right now
 I'll try it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: it's at your leisure.  The eyes are the window to the soul and if you make her promise to do it and keep eye contact. then that is a contract that cannot be broken

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Very true. It's how you trust them
 Thank you.
 bye

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am a very wealthy guy i just enjoy helping people.  have a nice day. EYE CONTACT!!!!!!

situation: My parents are making me going into a career path that I don't like.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hi there, how are you and how can i help?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
hey!
 not too bad but like i said
 im going into university and my parents want to me to do architecture
 when im really into engineering 
 money is a large factor so-

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i see. are your parents paying for your university?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
well money as in like salaries later on
 tuition fees aren't an issue im in canada and all
 but like architects generally make more money than mechanical engineers on average
 so they don't approve my career path

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: oh i see. so you mean that your parents would prefer you to choose the path that will eventually give you a higher salary?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yeah exactly!
 but i don't like architecture very much, i don't find myself fond of art

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: that is a tough situation. it's definitely not easy to go against your family's wishes, but at the same time you have to do what's best for you

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
well what's best for me is literally a good salary because i'd be lying if money isn't an issue

 but am i going to be happy later on doing the job?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i went through a similar experience during my schooling, and i had to explain to my parents that i don't want to disappoint them but i still have to do what makes me happy. having a career that you hate doesn't feel good - i've been there but there can be careers in engineering that have connections to architecture as well, such as structural engineering

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yeah i agree 
 it's much more suitable if i could mix up the details of each career paths
 but frankly i don't think my parents should be concerned with engineering either
 it pays well and provides good stability
 but like they said architecture suits my strengths more and i can go further with it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: honestly i think both are great career paths to pursue and as you said, both are stable and well-paying fields strengths doesn't always equal happiness though, so that's a tough one. have you considered speaking with both architects and engineers to learn more about each job? that might be helpful in your decision. sometimes what we think a job does, in actuality can be totally different in real life

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yeah!
 i went on reddit to ask students in my desired uni
 and they all said that what they are doing is what you expect
 architects said it's a booming business in Toronto
 engineers is always in demand, but not growing

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: ah well i commend you on taking that step and actually trying to learn about the jobs. you seem like a really smart person and you're able to see things from a logical perspective

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
thank you i've done the research to dispute my parents' arguments you see
 but like-
 utimately its about how much i like it o not
 *or

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i think what you need to do is take that same logical approach and explain calmly to your parents the path you want to take. just because they think your strengths are suited for architecture, doesnt mean you don't also have the strengths for engineering. many of the same skills are probably required for both jobs, and you're right: ultimately it is about what will make you happy and worst case scenario: you can always change careers later if you find that you don't like what you're doing

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yeah you are absolutely right
 but about the careers path
 not sure if you're in canada or not, but it's much harder to switch careers in canada
 you pay more and it's not like in the States where you can finish your degree whenever you want right
 once you paid

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you sound like a good student and i'm sure your parents must recognize that you're capable of great things, so if you can present to them a logical argument of why you feel engineering is a better path, hopefully they will listen.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yeah that is the plan
 you're been extremely helpful and im grateful for that
 i don't get to share these things very often
 my friends are just as busy too you see
 But I'll think we can stop it here

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you're welcome. best of luck to you and i hope you find the right path for yourself. i commend you for opening up here and i think you'll find success

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
It's nice to have someone motivate me hehe thank you so much
 best of luck to you too!
 once again thank you!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: thanks!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: bye!

situation: For the past couple years, I've been dealing with depression. I'm lonely, sad, scared and worried. I feel like there's no reason to live.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Hello
 Anyone there?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there. How are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Oh hello. I'm doing okay.
 How are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am well thanks, what can I help you with today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I came here because I am feeling quite hopeless. Life seems bleak. I feel as though there is nothing to live for and I will never be happy

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am so sorry to hear that. I understand, things can be tough sometimes.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I don't know how to be happy
 What do I do
 Ok

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you reached out to people who may be feeling the same? I know that has helped me when I have felt down. It is not easy, I know, but sometimes taking a small step can make all the difference

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't like to bother people who are feeling the same way. I don't want to be a burden on them

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand. Do you want to tell me more about what has been on your mind?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Sure. Everyday feels pointless. I wake up, do nothing, go back to sleep. Even when I do things it still feels pointless. I feel like life is meaningless and I would be better off dead. 
 I want to be happy, but I feel like I can never be happy. If I can never be happy, then what's the point of being alive?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I really do understand. It is very strong and brave of you to speak up. I have suffered depression myself in the past and one thing that truly helped was going to see my doctor and talking it though with him. He suggested medication and after some trial and error I found one that worked. You must be so strong to have dealt with the way you are feeling for so long. Please know there is help out there. I know from experience. You are a person of worth.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Oh my gosh you are an amazing person

 Thank you

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is very kind of you to say.

situation: I was really depressed for a really long time as I couldn't find a job whilst I was in college and this really effected me

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hey

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hi, how are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: not good

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: oh! what happen? Everything is ok

dialog.content: I have been feeling really depressed lately

Thank you and I am really stressed out with college and I really don't have a job so it is causing me stress as I college is difficult
 as it is now I don't have a job as well

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: ok i will, anyway thanks for the help, now i feel much better :)

dialog.content: thanks for sharing your feelings .Thanks

situation: I have a friend that completely screwed me out of money on a business venture.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, how are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I could be better. How about U?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am doing well. Whats bothering you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: A friend screwed me out of money and a business venture. I am no longer friends with her. We were really close before this.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh no! I am so sorry that has happened to you. That must feel like such a betrayal. Can you tell me more about it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
It does. I've known her for 14 years and we dated.
 I invested a lot of money in a business she wanted to do. She took the money and ran.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Dating can definitely complicate things.. Oh my gosh..... that is horrible. Surely there is something you can do to try to get your money back.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What do you suggest?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Did you sign anything? or have proof of what happened?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
No. Other than the money I transferred to her.
 Hello?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What was the business? It depends on your location but I'm sure just on the transaction alone that shows some kind of intent. or text messages saying it was for a specific purpose?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: A cat cafe. Where you can hang out and work or drink with adoptable cats from the local shelter.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If you gave her a large amount of money and have any kind of messages about it I would look into trying to get it back.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I gave her 80K. No messages. It was all done in person. Do you think I should get a lawyer and call the cops?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would seek the advice of a lawyer. I'm sorry this has happened thats horrible.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It is. I don't want to go to the police. I think they are all corrupt and raciest.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would say 80K is worth speaking to a lawyer about it. I'm sorry you are so conflicted. I would not call the police because you willingly gave it to her. I would hire a lawyer to handle it.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's great advice. I will do that. I have a mutual friend that is a lawyer.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would not talk to a mutual friend about this at all....

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Ok. Thanks for all the advise.

situation: Ever since the insurgent of Covid-19 ,I was hit hard when I was relieved from my full-time job for a non justifiable reason. I blame it to Covid because business was slow and it affected production. I was so frustrated and I didn't know what next to do as I had past due bills and family to take care of. I started applying for government benefits none was fast coming forth and requires a lot of processing time. I later got a part-time job that the pay was low and i could not put up with the bills .it was really tough. I almost went into depression but I did not give up home. Recently I got a full-time job but I have been scared and anxious because they keep laying off employees  weekly. I wouldn't want to go through the terrible situation I had last time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi! How are you feeling today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am feeling better  How are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am good. How may I assist you today!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I would love some words of encouragement

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: IT seem you need some encouragement to accomplish your goal. Hope I understood it correctly.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great! We all need motivation and encouragement to do something.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Have you been in a situation like mine before?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have been  in this situation many time. I perform better when I get support or encouraging words from other. Can you please tell me little bit about your goal, so that I can encourage you accordingly

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I always want a situation where i work without anxiety and fear of being forloughed because of my last experience

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand your situation very well. We all face such a phase in our life time. you should not worry about it. Keep working hard.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I put in extra work just to be on top of my goals

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great! Then you should not worry about anything. Now days situation is really stressful. We have to manage it somehow. You should give little time to yourself as well. It Will increase your productivity.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My family is my greatest motivation to keep pushing hard .Time heals all wounds just well said

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Family is a great support, then you will definitely grow and you don't have to take any stress because you have everything there with you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Do you have dependents that look up to you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, I have kids. To look them up I need to stay motivated and positive because I am the reflection to them.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that is true I feel relieved chating with you Thank you for your words of encouragements. It really helped a lot

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's my pleasure that my words helped you. Stay happy calm and motivated and rest you have big support of your family.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Enjoy the rest of your day.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too! byee

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: bye.

situation: my girlfriend dumped me. sad.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there, how is life treating you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: terrible, i got dumped. the other day. im alone during a pandemic.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Your partner has left you? I am very sorry to hear that. How long were you together for?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: 7 years

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow that is a really long time. I can understand why you are feeling so lousy. Is there any chance of reconciliation?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
probably not. or at least i dont see that happening in the near future
 they cheated on me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I remember when my partner left me after eight years I felt that I was never going to get over it. I was angry and miserable for a very long time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thats what im expecting to be. oh well, at least with the year we have all had i can just add this to the pile.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes it has been a really terrible year for all of us. I am so sorry that this happened to you on top of everything else!  I know how bad it feels but I can promise you that it will fade after time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thanks, its been tough. i still have my dog at least.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah dogs are absolutely wonderful companions aren't they? Especially when the world is so isolated right now

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: she is super cuddly. this would be extremely hard without her.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What breed of dog is she? I have two cats and they have been really helping me when I've been feeling low.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i actually dont know. she's a rescue from mexico. she's a mix breed. maybe some whipit and lab. she looks like dobby from harry potter lol

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Rescues are the best. My two are rescues and it took them a while to trust me but now they love me unconditionally. It is good that you have a pet in your life .. much better than your useless ex I think!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: haha yep. i think my dog is thrilled with having the extra bed space, as mean as that sounds

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: LOL well it's good that you are able to look on the bright side about this. Have you considered making plans for the future, once the pandemic is over?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yep, ill be getting back to my life and plans after. i have a bad heart condition so ive had to stay inside pretty much all year.  also another messed up thing my ex did to me, cheated on me and then came back into the house. she could have passed it to me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear about your heart condition, that obvious compounds your problems. I believe that things are going to improve in the New Year though  Let's face it, there does come a time when it seems that not much can get worse

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: and thats when it gets worse haha

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Try to look on the bright side of things :) At least you can't be dumped twice ;)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: haha thats true. thats comforting thanks

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hope that one day you feel that you will be able to date and trust another person though. Don't let one bad apple put you off for life.  Anyway it has been very nice to chat, I hope that I was able to be of help

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: totally, thanks.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Enjoy the rest of your day!

situation: I have anger issues which has made me lose a lot of friends. I really need someone to help me get out of this before I hurt myself.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello Nice meeting you

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there, how is your day going?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Fine and yours?  I have anger issue

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Uh oh, you feel you have an anger issue?  What's going on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: How can you help me resolve it?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there anyone you can talk to about what you are going through?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I get angry easily and it has affected a lot of my relationships.  I don't seem to have anyone to talk to.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How do you feel it is affecting your relationships?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I get angry at the slightest provocation.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand how it can feel like you have no one to talk to.  Are you in school age, maybe a counselor could help?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm not getting it funny anymore

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What do you mean?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm an adult Im a single mom

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I was angry for a period of time after my husband passed away and I get where you are coming from I am also a single mom now

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's glad you can see the world from my perspective. How did you overcome it?  Oh that makes the 2 of us.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Day by day, focusing on my kids and how I want them to be happy.  I also found a great support group in my community.  Have you checked in to something like that?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm thinking of something like that. I guess loneliness also makes it worse for me. I live alone...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know how hard it can be to live and feel alone.  Do you have any pets?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't have a pet but thinking of getting one.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think that's a great idea!  My dogs have really helped, they provide great comfort.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will get a cat 🙀 then. I heard they are a great companion.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds like a really good start in helping with your loneliness, it's great that you are thinking of ideas that will help move you forward.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes you've been great too. I love how you helped me handle my issues. You're awesome.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you, that's nice to hear.   Would you be interested in looking at the library for resources on groups or counseling?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes that's another great idea. I'm gonna check on our local library to get some books 📚

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If you feel less comfortable in person, I bet there are many groups and information online as well.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Where are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: At home. Yesterday was great weather here.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Okay 👌

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have some errands to run, but I hope our chat helped and I hope you start feeling better.  I think your idea to get a cat and also to check out some resources is a great start in helping you heal.

situation: Tomorrow is my daughter's birthday, my oldest daughter who I gave up 37 years ago. I sent her a letter a year ago, at least it is believed that this is her from searcher that helped me. She has not responded to that or other requests from me.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: good morning

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi , how are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: tired, how about you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Good afternoon  Why your tired?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Is it afternoon where you are?

 I'm still waking up, should have more coffee. And, I am just always tired, tired of everything these days

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes , What happen why you are tired please can you explain  ok , everything going ok

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think it's my depression. I have a very hard time getting motivated to do anything. Nothing seems worth it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Please can you tell why you are in depression ?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I have suffered with it all my life, but this past year has made it so much worse. I feel as if I have no control over anything as these stupid lockdowns go on and on and on
 Are you a real person?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: please engaged yourself in different actives that help you come out from this depression

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I know that I need to do that, but it's pushing myself that is so hard.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: yes, everyone going in this situation because of lock down

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: For sure, and I know that so many are much worse off than I.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: yes i know that's hard put please try to engaged yourself in fun activates

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: what do you do for fun? can you give me any ideas?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: those activities that you like that you feel fun in it here is also lock down situation from log time , how is there? Why your in depression because of job or any thin else  thing*

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Same, wish I could move to another state where they have more freedoms. Also, it is still winter here so that does not help. I spend my time worrying about those hurt thru all of this...so many having depression, anxiety, increased overdoses, suicides and relapses.
 I have always had this depression, think it must be a chemical thing, I don't know. I just never feel happy, since i was a kid
 Quit my job recently because I was working helping people filing their unemployment and it was so stressful!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: why please don't feel like that i hope you will come out from this very soon

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I feel other's pain so much
 thank you :) you sound like a nice person

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: yes , Thanks

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: can you tell me where you are? I am in the US, in Illinois

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm in London

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I hope you are real. It is good to connect with someone else!
 Cool!!! I would love to hear your accent, lol

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: yes I'm real and i hope everyone come out from this

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
At least there is sun out today. How about there?
 It's been nice chatting with you. I hope you have a good day! Do something fun

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: cloudy, windy as always here

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: haha...sorry about that! Be safe and hang in there. It's got to get better someday. It's on the way

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thanks , i hope i help you in all this your situation

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
You have! Just to talk to someone is good. Thank you!
 bye for now

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: your welcome, bye

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: have a nice day, and weekend....bye

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too, bye

situation: Due to this pandemic, I fear for my job.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi there, how's it going?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, how are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not so good, you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that. What is going on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: This pandemic has me stressed about my job stability.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you are worried you may lose your job due to the pandemic, is that correct?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes. My firm has already cut 15%. I am fearful I'll be next since I'm a low level employee.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It seems to me from chatting with you that you are extremely anxious about the situation.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, very. If I were to get fired. I have no idea what to do for my future.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I completely understand as I was in the same situation when the pandemic started and I wasn't sure if I was going to keep my job.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Where I live, we currently have a lock down. I feel like small businesses will be forced to shut down. Economy is getting worse. My anxiety is getting worse.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What industry do you currently work in?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Finance.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know this is an extremely hard situation, but I know you can make it through it!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I doubt it. But thanks for the positive vibes.
 Is there a lock down where you live?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Yes, we are currently in a lock down.
 Have you had a talk with your supervisor about the situation?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, but he can only do so much to be honest.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Something I used to do when I was struggling with anxiety over this was come home and play video games, which is something I really enjoy doing. Have you tried to really dive into more activities that you enjoy?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do sometimes. Which games do you recommend?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I play a lot of sports games, mostly racing.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh, that's very interesting. Thank you.

situation: I am qualified for a promotion but my boss won't promote me

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm qualified for a promotion but my boss is refusing to promote me based on biased feedback he has received from someone else

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like you are having a hard time because your boss isn't willing to consider you for a promotion, is that right?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes that's right. It's frustratgn
 *frustrating

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you talked to anyone else at work about it? I was in a similar situation once, I confided in a co-worker that I trusted and it gave me a different perspective.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Unfortunately 
I can't really talk to anyone at work because there is no one I trust to this extent. Promotions are a bit of a sensitive issue

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you reported to your HR department the issue around biased feedback being used as a determining factor in not being considered?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: The HR department favours managers so I am worried that will damage my chances of promotion further

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sorry you have to deal with this at work. I think you deserve the promotion because you are very dedicated.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks, that's good to hear

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think if you continue to advocate for yourself in a positive manner you could possibly increase the chances of being considered for the promotion.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's hard to be positive when I feel like my manager is not receptive to my opinions and just trusts the feedback given by this one other person

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I've worked at a place that seems similar to your work environment. My co-workers were not supportive and I was always overlooked. Eventually, I realized that the environment was toxic and chose to start applying for different jobs. I felt like if my potential couldn't be appreciated then I should go somewhere with opportunities for growth.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Maybe it is time for me to start looking elsewhere for better opportunities. I've worked at this place so long that I have become comfortable here and the thought of searching for a new job is intimidating

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You've got this! It's easy to become comfortable in what we know but, change causes us to grow.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah I agree, it's uncomfortable and difficult though! I haven't searched for a new job in so long, I feel like I will perform terribly at the interviews

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Just start slowly and remember that you can be picky about what position you chose. I also think we all feel like we perform badly during interviews. Preparation is key. I hope discussing your work situation today has made you feel a bit better!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It has, thanks for being supportive and listening to my problem

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It was my pleasure! Thank you for sharing with me. I'm glad I had the opportunity.

situation: I have an interview with little time to prepare and I'm feeling very stressed about this as I tend to get very nervous at interviews.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi there

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm not feeling great. I applied for a job recently which took a lot of time. I now have an interview and it's in 3 days. I'm getting really nervous about this because I think I need longer to prepare.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know how you feel, I have often felt anxiety before a job interview.  What makes you feel that you are unprepared?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: The thing is I'm really good at interviews but usually it takes time to prepare. It's a corporate interview so I need to prepare behavioural answers to certain scenarios. In the past this has taken me a week to learn and record to memory

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is it possible that you could get a friend or family member to work with you on your responses? That could possibly speed up your preparation time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes perhaps. I need to draw on previous experience so I think I'll need to prepare. I'm just so busy at work these days I just don;t think I'll have the time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can see that you are feeling a large amount of stress about the situation. Would it be possible to take one or more days off from work to give yourself more time?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes this is very stressful. Have you ever had a similar experience and if so how did you handle it?
 Potentially but this is very short notice, usually I have to provide more notice for leave from work.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, I have had to deal with time pressures quite a few times. I am sure most people have in the modern era where everything moves so fast. I have usually gotten help from friends, or done my best to delay or remove other commitments from my life. As a last resort, I have sometimes had to accept that I would be getting less sleep than usual for a few days.  Are there any other commitments that you can possibly delay or remove from your life while you prepare for your job interview?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Perhaps I can ask if I can finish early from work for the next couple of days and get some time that way. I do think however I'll be more nervous due to less time to prepare. The thing is I'd delay the interview but this might affect my chances.
 I suppose I could ask my partner to look after the childcare for the next couple of days.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That seems like a good idea, unless that will be likely to cause you other problems. You seem like a hard working, diligent type of person that can make this work if you make the effort.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Tanks , I think I need to stay positive about this. Not sure how to handle my interview nerves however?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, everyone handles stressful situations differently, but there are some things you can do or try that might help. If you prepare as much as possible in the time that you have, you can be confident that you have done your best, and that is all that matters. You can decide that while you would like to get this new job, the world will not end if you don't get it, and you will worry less and have less stress. Also there is the classic technique of picturing your interviewer in their underwear. It makes them seem less imposing, and you can feel more confident. Do you think any of these ideas might help?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: There are actually some great suggestions there. The naked idea is a classic and it did make me smile. I think I am aiming for perfection when I think I should try and relax and just give it my best shot. That does help actually!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Be confident in your abilities. You are the right person for the job, and if the interviewer does not see that, it is their failing, not yours. You will do great.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for that. I think the key to this is confidence so I'll go with it. Feeling more relaxed about it all now,

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Excellent. I am very happy to hear that. Is there anything else I can help you with today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No that's great. Thanks from your help

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are quite welcome. It was a pleasure speaking with you. Have a great day.

situation: I was given reduced hours at work and there might be a chance I could get laid off because I just started and there is lack of work. However, my expenses are the same and income is reducing.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Not too bad!
 It looks like a lovely day out.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Well
 make sure you're bundled up and warm!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Sounds cozy. Glad to hear that you're staying safe indoors!

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker

Not too bad, still have limited work and all remote so wont complain. But the expenses are tough.
 How about you, hope your well

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm not too bad, but cases are also going up here in California. Luckily, my family and I haven't gotten sick.

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No gifts giving and going make a modest size home made family dinner for thanksgiving this year. Usually we go visit family out state. We just want to get out of debt this year and save for next year.  Coivd really humbled us. But it's a good thing

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Those are all really good ideas, and it sounds like you know what's really important! It's definitely been a tough year, but it's also good to look ahead and plan for the future.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, thanks for the chat. Love the positive vibes!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It's been great chatting with you too! Hope you and your loved ones stay safe out there!

situation: My supervisor lied about having adequate coverage.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How may I help you today?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Why?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My supervisor said she would be in to help cover people on PTO and she never showed.

dialog.content: So your boss never showed up to cover people on PTO is that correct?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Did anybody ever get in contact with her and to see why she never showed up?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes, she said she decided she was also taking PTO. 
 Even though she had already said she would help cover.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand how you are feeling because i would be angry to I feel like she should of let everyone know that before she decided to do that

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Agreed, but at this point I just need to get over it so I can help pick up the slack. 
 But i'm just so upset.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you personally had a conversation with her and tell her how you was feeling?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, i'm afraid to bring it up.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Don't be afraid to bring it up because you did nothing wrong

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: But what if she gets mad?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I don't see why she would get mad but if she does then just leave it alone and move on and just do your job That is what i would do

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yea, I guess so.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I honestly do not think she will get mad just try to talk to her  I would just let it go because to me it's not worth being mad over for a long time you have to just let it go

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think I will. I guess i'm worried because I had already expressed my concern with having multiple people on PTO and she specifically said, "it's okay because I can help cover"  So she just completely disregarded what I said.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand she should of talked to you about changing her mind

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: So is it even worth bringing it up at all?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I mean you know it won't hurt to try to bring it up and see what happens seems to me your boss is not doing her job right

situation: I've suffered from depression since I was a teenager.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hey, hi are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm okay i guess. How are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm pretty good, I can't complain. Is there any thing going on with your life that you would like to discuss?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My marriage recently fell apart and i had to relocate to NC with my children. That's going as well as you would think it is.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know separation isn't easy, especially with children involved. How are you coping with this transition?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I guess im okay, a little shaky and still find myself in tears from time to time. Honestly it is nothing new for me. I've suffered from depressions since i was a young teen

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well breakup is not easy so what you're feeling is normal. Have you ever sought out professional help for depression?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I go to the local mental health. I'm actually bipolar 1 which kinda makes my depression a little worse. I see a counselor twice a month. I still however have some trouble in between visits. I just can not afford to be seen more often.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are there hobbies or activities that you do that help you cope with issues such as jogging, reading, etc.?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I read a lot and play rpg games to distract myself. My kids are a big help too. ill admit there are some days despite trying to distract myself i stuggle.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Its good to see that you have activities you participate in that helps you cope. I also play video games to just get away mentally too. But yes, even hobbies are not the cure for issue but rather helps with the symptoms.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh really what kinda video games to you escape into? I like RPG, romance, adventure, and simulation

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I love to play shooters and sports lol. I will be getting into the VR world very soon.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh i love some shooters. But VR scares me honestly. I heard it can cause seizures

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm scared as well as it may feel too realistic for me.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yea god forbid playing a shooter in VR O.o

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hahaha...Have you tslked to family and friends about the situation? How did they respond and what suggestions did they offer?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well my mama suggested i just forget my ex and not be depressed. My grandpa suggested once i get divorced i should just move on. My brother asked if i wanted him to beat his ass XD lol So not much help honestly

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you want the relationship to be over or you are truly done and want to move on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I realize i am glad its over. I realize i did not love him like i thought i did. I'm sad that my babies had to suffer for me to realize this.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Although it's tough for the children, it's positive to hear that maybe it was for the best. The family seemed to echo your same feelings so it could be for the best.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I certainly hope so. I wonder sometimes. I hope i haven't ruined my kids lives by leaving their father.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Some children may act out if they have trouble expressing how they feel. I would suggest having regular talks with them and being transparent.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes we had a little trouble with my 8 year old. He thought i and his grandma chased his daddy away. He acted out a lot till we set down with him and explained what happened.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh wow, well its good that an explanation was there for him to understand. As time goes on, it will become easier

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I sure hope so. Thanks so much for your time. You really made me feel better about the situation. I actually enjoyed this hit

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No problem, the pleasure was all mines!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: ^-^ have a good day sugar!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too, thanks!

I agree with that. I just hope 
 *

Or you could start your own business as you have already tried as a YouTuber. I heard a lot of people starts their own because of Covid

dialog.content:
I never thought about adding a picture to my resume, but it would help show that I'm a profes
sional. I have ran my own business, doing custom woodworking for people. For now, I'd rather work for other people. Plus the whole being broke thing really restricts what I could do for a new business.

situation: I've had trouble sleeping the last few nights, I feel really low self esteem.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there, how are you feeling at the moment?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, not totally terrible, but sad.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that but I am happy to listen. What would you like to talk about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I used to be really driven to succeed at things. After I graduated college and tried jobs that lasted 1-2 years each at most before I was fired, I gave up believing in most of my abilities to succeed in the workforce

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay, well, let's take this one thing at a time. What would you say the main reason you were fired was?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Now, even during the pandemic, I have no drive to re-enter the workforce and my family thankfully isn't pushing me to get a job, but I often doubt my self worth because I haven't felt successful at long term goals in a few years

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand how it feels to feel you are not doing as well as you might. I work in the gig economy and enjoy it but it's not what I'd intended to do with my life.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, the last company I worked at, we were all fired....because the landlord raised the rent so hire on my boss the owner, he couldn't afford to stay there, pre-pandemic

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hmph that does not sound at all cool but it can hardly be considered your fault.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
In fact most of the jobs I've had, the companies have been sold. In retrospect it's easy for me to realize I should have gone to more stable companies that weren't eventually 
sold Also I suffer from what Google seems to call imposter syndrome

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It's a very unstable time unfortunately - even highly reputable companies seem to be in trouble.  Ah I have heard of that. You do not think that you are good enough?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, I haven't felt the desire I used to feel before the pandemic to go get a new job because I felt burned out working in retail for minimum wage

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hear you. It's a demoralising sector to work in, for sure. What would you like to do, if you had your choice?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I used to do a lot of charity work as a side hobby to help other people, and sadly, the pandemic kind of spotlit how many of us are struggling and broke, so it didn't exactly feel proper asking to help other peopole in wheelchairs like myself who I'd raise money for to go to adaptive summer camp (kids and adults) - this'll be the 2nd summer in a row where camp is virtual
 I never wanted to be paid for that charity work btw

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know that :) You seem like a good person.  I think that the pandemic has had a negative impact on virtually everyone. It's been a very strange 15 months, for sure.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I wanted to finish writing my autobiography as a poetry book, and slowly, over the last two to three years, I began doubting myself and stopped writing because it seems like the entire American population is so easily upset and make
s everything political

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, you could still finish it just for your own sake? It would be something worthwhile to look back on later.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think I WILL eventually follow through with the book....I have to get over the fear of Twitter hating my personal experience even if they dosagree woth me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would stay away from twitter .. it 's very toxic.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
It sure would be, I mean, before the Trump era 5 years ago, I just wanted my dad and sister to have something they could share with future relatives 50 years from now about me n
stead of a sad three paragraph obituary

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I don't think I'd even get two paragraphs to be honest. But keep writing it, don't give up.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Twitter is the worst, I left there in 2017 I think, and that was a good move. Sadly, everyone seems to be divided on every platform these days. Again, it SEEMS that way even if it really isn't
 You're right. It's my own fears that ultimately I have to conquer

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think people just have more of a platform to vent and feel that they can be hateful because no-one can see them.  Anyway it has been nice to talk to you .. I hope I have been able to help you!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Because let's say I did die (heaven forbid) sometime soon.....I would feel heartbroken taking my last breath knowing that I didn't tell the world what I lived

 Thanks for your advice / listening. Really appreciated it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're very welcome :) Can you press the quit button first, I can't this end :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh, sorry, lol.....sure thing

Hello.
 Hello?

dialog.content:
Understandable

dialog.content: Yes, my husband is very supportive but I don't want to upset him too much so I don't fully open up.

dialog.content: I will look into the telehealth. Thank you for that recommendation.

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will give that a try. Thank you again.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there anything else you would like to talk about or that I can help you with today?

Hello, I am currently going through a bad breakup with my boyfriend.
 Hello

Yeah that's somewhat of the boat I'm now in.

dialog.content: That's a good idea. I need to talk with people who understand and who just won't tell me to get over it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Absolutely.  I think things will turn around for you.  You have a lot going for you and are very intelligent.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you so much. I hope so! I know this will pass.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Things will get better with time.  Keep pursuing your dreams Is there anything else I can help you with?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Good question! Don't let the feeling consume you. After all , you are dealing with another person who genuinely might be busy or held up with some important work.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thats true

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So, even if they don't respond, just think they are driving or working, and try to avoid thinking that you are being ignored.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I always assume since im not busy that they arent too I guessits a little self involved

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, sometimes assumptions can lead to misunderstandings. Always talk it out when you get a chance. It could be, but always bear in mind that there could be factors beyond your immediate control.

situation: I recently argued with my family about my dating partner.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello how are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi. How are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Doing good and yourself?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I’ve been better. Just got into a major argument with my parents.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That can be tough can  you tell me what the argument was about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: They don’t agree with the girl I have been dating.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So, why do you think they think that?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Because of her race. She is black.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can see why you would be upset about that. So, that is the only reason they don't approve. At this point when they are not as upset you may want to share all the reason she a great girlfriend. If it is based totally on race not sure you can change their minds.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You are right. But I’m afraid it is solely based on race. She is such a great person. I wish they could look past her race.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I completely understand that and if you really like her and they see how happy she makes you maybe they will come around.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I hope so. But they are old school. I just lost my cool.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would be upset too so, it's understandable.  Just reassure them you love them and your happy and hopefully one day they will be happy with your decesion. If not then it is your decesion who you date and I think they know that too.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Have you dated interracial?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes  I have and my parents did not approve either Eventually my parents came around and most likely yours will too.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What race was it? What did you do?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It was in highschool I dated a black man after I became an adult they didn't say much about who I dated and I countinued to date the person and it ended when he went in the miltary and I went to college not at first but it was the distance thing.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Interesting. My girl is black. I hope they come around too.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Most likely they will if she makes you happy they will see that :-)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You have been a great help!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Glad that I could be and good luck to you

situation: I think I've drifted apart from a friend I've known since college because of the pandemic. We rarely communicate and the last time we did she tells me about the things she has been doing with her NEW friends.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi! What's on your mind tonight?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think a friend I've known for 30 years since college has drifted away from me.  Even during the pandemic she has made new friends.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh, I'm sorry, that's not fun at all, especially since you two have been friends for so long.  Have you tried reaching out to her by phone, email or social media?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
She has cut our conversations short by saying she is tried. She will return text but I feel like I am always initiating.
 She likes watching tv show and told me with her NEW friend they Facetime and watch TV together.  I really hate watching TV so don't want to offer to participate.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, it's a positive sign that she hasn't blocked you completely. I agree, texting her each day could irritate her.  That's okay! You shouldn't do anything that isn't fun or enjoyable or comfortable for either of you, in my opinion.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am jealous that she is more successful in making new friends. I have not made a new friend in over 10 years.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like perhaps you should focus your attention on other friends and hopefully eventually this friend will need you for something in thier life

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't have many other friends and they are out of state.  The one we are talking about lived an hour away but we would see each other 4 to 6 times a year,

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you're saying you feel hurt and depressed at not being able to make new friends easily in the pandemic, correct? Ahhhh.,.....yes that;s difficult, oh boy.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hurt, mad, jealous. I have trouble getting out enough to make friends - even if there wasn't a pandemic. I work a lot.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: For the time being we're all stuck at home mostly, so maybe take some comfort in that in a strange way. Heck I rarely see my friends - maybe once a year, so I know how that feels.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Good to know I'm not alone.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: On the bright side of things, at least you have a job. Do you enjoy your job? Maybe start friendships with coworkers, possibly.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Work place is very small and I'm not in the clique there. Vaccine will be available to my age group soon and the weather is getting warmer. So there is that to look forward too.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How often do you get out for fresh air? Sure it's not going to solve the lack of friends but it does help to clear your mind to go outside the next few months after work.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Good idea. I really hope being able to take walks will help me clear my thoughts.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, I agree with you, the warmer weather with the vaccine will probably speed up the end of all this. Cabin fever is real.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I may join the book club when the library reopens.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It's really hard for people in our situation right now because there's only so many virtual things to attend but maybe I just thought of a virtual cocktail hour, and you could go on Facebook or even meetup.com or a free dating site to connect with other people in our situation who live near you. The book club is a great idea.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Good ideas.  Thank you.  It was nice chatting with you.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Same here, wish I could've been more helpful but we're in similar situations and nothing else came to my mind. Hang in there, you're doing great under the circumstances!

situation: Depression exacerbated by nasty migraines lately.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there. How are things treating you at the moment?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Could be better or worse, how are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Pretty much sums it up really. I think that that is how a lot of us are feeling right now. Is there anything you'd like to talk about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have been getting migraines lately which is not fun.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ouch I hear you .. I have suffered from those for years.  They are no fun at all are they?  Are you getting any sort of treatment for it? There are some effective treatments out there.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I actually just talked to my doctor today, I am going to track my triggers and see if we can figure out why I am getting them. Also some meds for the symptoms.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds really productive. Do you think that the attacks might be stress related in some way?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It could be but they seem to happen more on weekends so that doesn't really make sense.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hmm yes I can see how that does not make any sense. Do you have any other medical issues?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No I am in pretty good health, a bit of sciatica from a few years back but that only flares up occasionally.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, I believe that there are some new advances in migraine treatment at the moment so I think things will improve for you. I am sorry if I am not being more help.  You sound as  though you are dealing with the problem pretty well though

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks I knoq its pretty hard to fix medical issues with a conversation, I am also a little depressed if that helps:)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah I hear you on that. Do you feel that this is related to the pandemic or is it something that has been ongoing for a while?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No this is a very old friend

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: There are some excellent counselling services available if you are interested in going that route.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Honestly I have a pretty good handle on it, I have done a lot of therapy and I am quite open with people about it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is a really healthy attitude to have. I think that you are dealing with this very well .. well done!  Back to migraines, have you considered trying to alter your diet to help with it? Some people believe that avoiding certain foods can be really helpful.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I need to start keeping track of what triggers me, Caffeine is definitely off the menu because that sets it off for me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
That's a shame - I would really miss caffeine if I had to do without it! I did try cutting out sugar but it did not really have any effect 
 I hope that you can find some resolution in the New Year. All the best to you and thanks for talking!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for your help, Have a wonderful day!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too! Don't forget to press the button and take the survey ;)

situation: I am getting ready to sue my mother for not believing my step dad raped me.

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.content: I am getting ready to sue my mother and am very nervous about the ordeal.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're worried about being in court against your mother or are you worried about your relationship after that?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Both

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Can you tell me about the situation, why did you decide to sue her?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My stepdad raped me and got me pregnant and I delivered a still born and i just remember the incident happened 23 years later. So now I am suing my mother. instead of pressing charges because my sister and I dont want it in the papers

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So she knew about the incident but did nothing to help you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes she did not believe me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: that sucks, I understand why you resent her so much I cannot help with your worry about going to court, however, i can offer some help regarding your relationship

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes it is very difficult but im not as upset as i was i cried a lot

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Crying is good, you shouldn't bottle your emotions Have to talked to anyone in your family about this beside your sister?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yes i am kinda numb to it but I have to call the police dept tomorrow for a copy of the police report from when my step dad took the baby to them and I dont want to
 No nobody knows except one cousin from my biological dads side

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You should talk to someone you trust, they might be able to give you specific advice regarding your situation

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.content: If you still care about her, make sure to let her know you don't hate or resent her and you still hope to be able to fix your relationship

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i will tell her thank you

situation: I work a dead end job.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, can I help you today? I am available to listen and help you think through some things. I know it can be difficult to express how we are feelings sometimes.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It can be difficult to express feelings. I agree.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you have a specific problem that you are experiencing?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I work a dead end job with no potential and it is depressing being there for 9 hours a day. Thats why I am on here instead of working.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds really exhausting. You work longer hours than a normal job and must feel really burned out.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah the burn out isn't the worst I experienced. I previously worked jobs that I was at for 12 hours a day but they were more rewarding.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are definitely a hard worker and I hope you know that. You make a great employee puttin in som many hours.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am there for 9 hours. I probably only work for 1 hour a day. The rest of the time I am killing time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I was in a job that I felt was a waste of time and I was able to find another one eventually. I understand how you can feel. Do you have any plans to look for different work in the future?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No. I have no energy to find a new job. Also given the pandemic, keeping a secure paying job is more important than happiness.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can relate to that feeling too. You are exhausted and feeling like you cant get out of a rut sort of?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes you hit the nail on the head. In a rut for sure.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am glad that you are expressing yourslef today and facing your feelings. This could be a start to changing somethings in your life. It isn't usually easy talking about being in a rut.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It is very tough to express feelings.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would like to suggest starting a journal to write down just the bullet points of how you feel at the end of each day. Would you consider trying this activity?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No. It has been suggested to me before. While I understand the benefits, it is something that I don't feel like doing.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It can help to review your own thoughts dad to day and attempt to find a pattern that you may not be aware of in your life.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's a good point about recognizing patterns.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You could always start with just taking 5 minutes per day and after a week quit it and reach back out and we can try something else.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is a good idea. I also see a psychologist and a psychiatrist for help.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is very productive. We have a lot of ideas for therapy and can work on finding the best one for you if journaling is a fail. Thank you for reaching out today!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for taking the time to listen.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome. Take care!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too. Have a nice day.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you! I am available if you have anything else on your mind or you can kindly use the provided feature to finish/quit this session today. Hope to hear from you again soon.

When I had an argument with my friend, we both knew it was because we cared deeply for each other. I'm sure you are a very loyal and honest friend who just feels comfortable enough to disagree with your other friends!

Yes. Some movie talks going on starts with and then going to personal think touch my ego

situation: My dad and I do not talk anymore even though we used to be close.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hey there, what's going on today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello. It is a fine day, how about yourself?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It really is! I am just taking in the fresh air in the backyard.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is awesome.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thanks! What is going on in your neck of the woods?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Sort of stuck inside trying to make a dime but it is alright.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah great minds think alike.. do you do anything else for work?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Looking for a good job right now.
 I wanted to talk about a personal issue I have been having, though.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay, you know I'm here for you. Talk to me, what's happening?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My father and I had a fight 2 years ago and haven't spoke since.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: The same happened to me, and then my father passed away. If there is anything I can say, is to swallow pride and cherish the moments you have on earth together. What was the fight over?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah I am afraid that these things can happen. It was only over material things.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That was my next question, what the fight was over. Do you think he would be open to mending things?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It is weird because I called him out for being manipulative so this is his defense.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Usually when people feel singled out, they only have a few mechanisms. Unfortunately, most people tend to run away instead of fixing things. It tends to be the easier option.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I would likely take advantage of an opportunity to be the bigger person.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: When you try to fix things, just remember that this is his method. I wouldn't hold back on what you have to say, but just word it so that he doesn't feel the need to flee.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is probably decent advice.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It is HIS responsibility as a parent to want to fix things, but you are also being the bigger person here. I applaud you and hope that he is receptive.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I hope so too, but the opportunity seems tenuous

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How did you guys leave it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Threatening and disparagement haha

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ha, well after two years, feelings had to have changed. Even a little. Does he live nearby?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah someday. I think I will have to try you know. THanks for the advice.
