situation: I am having a hard time sleeping due to being nervous about the future

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, I'm here to listen. What's going on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Thank you. I've lost my job and I'm very nervous about the future
 As a result I am having a hard time sleeping

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow, that's very hard. I would be anxious too.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That makes me feel a lot better. Have you ever lost your job?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have. I was laid off. It was nerve-racking, but also really sad.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What helped you get through it?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I had a bit in savings, but I looked for new jobs mostly. I was on unemployment insurance for about a month before I found a new job. Have you made any plans yet about how to move forward? I can offer some suggestions if you would like. I know about a lot of resources

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I am a gig worker, so I cannot apply for IU. Its very hard, but i also have some savings.
 What kind of tools would you recommend I use to get through this

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, falling back on savings while you look for more work is good for a while. If you have any food insecurity as a result, there are often local food banks. It's really not something to be embarrased about, so many people face food insecurity through no fault of their own. You could also give a call to your local social services because they will be able to tell you what resources would be available, whether it's a vocational program or temporary assistance.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Wow you're so helpful and encouraging! It's very nice to talk to you. 
 Are there any meds you can recommend to help me sleep?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, if it's possible, I would talk to a doctor. If you're looking for Over-the-counter sleep medication, melatonin has helped me in the past.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Wow! I've never used melatonin before. Did it really help you? Are you doing much better now?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
It defiitely helps me fall asleep.
 It's a natural sleep aid.
 If something like that doesn't work, I would talk to a doctor. I know money will be tight though. Are there any free clinics near you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I'll look into it. There's a lot of services in the general area that might be able to help me
 It's been really nice talking to you

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you. I hope you're feeling a bit better. Is there anything else you want to talk about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No I'm okay. I think that's everything :)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Alright, well I hope you have a good night. Good luck, you've got this!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too :) Thanks <3

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Bye!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: bye

situation: i feel like my life is in a rut

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: HI, I need help, I have been feeling very down lately.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay, how can I help you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I just wanted to talk to someone.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there anything particular that has got you feeling down lately?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I feel like I am in a rut.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Its always good to have someone to talk to, I am happy to help. I have felt stuck many times, so i can understand that.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What did you do to get out of the rut.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well I had to sit down and think about why I was stuck and tried to come up with as many options as I could to be unstuck, then i put them in order based on which I thought would work best. It took quite a few of them, but I did finally get unstuck.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I can try doing that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Every one is different, but it is worth a shot, might help the rut your having, might also give you new ideas that will help you decide on how to not feel like your in a rut anymore. Sometimes just talking to someone can help too, bouncing ideas off of each other can help in some cases.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: True,  I have been avoiding most people.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Its hard to talk about certain problems, so I can't blame you, however you can get some good ideas from others. You might have just needed some space to evaluate your situation, nothing wrong with taking a break from things or people if it helps you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will try reaching out to my sister tomorrow.  maybe she can help. I also feel embarrassed that I feel so unhappy.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
From chatting with you I can tell you might be ready to start talking and maybe get some help or advice, family is a great start they are typically a great support system. It is okay to feel embarrassed but being unhappy will eat at you, sometimes its good to set our pride aside so that we can figure things out. In my situation of feeling stuck I was ashamed that I sat and let people treat me so badly but it wasn't until I seen it as a problem that I started making my lists and getting advice. Some things we do on our own and some its good to have people to help. I think reaching out to your sister would be worth a try, she might also have different ideas and suggestions you haven'
t thought of yet.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks, you have been a lot of help.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you for chatting with me, I am glad to help. I hope you have a great day! Stay positive!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will try and you have a nice day too. Bye

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If you need to chat again I would be glad to talk to you, I think you will do great at fixing your rut. Thank you.  Bye.

situation: I can not seem to find a job no matter how hard I try. My last job was cut short because of all the pandemic restrictions. I'm so depressed.

dialog.content: Hello. How are you?

dialog.content: i am good how can i help you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I can't seem to find a job...I've been trying so hard. I will run out of money, soon...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i can understand in this time it is hard finding a job during this pandemic

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have a BA but that doesn't seem to help me find a job, much. Seems like all I see are jobs for construction type jobs.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: have you tried looking online for jobs?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, it's the main way I search. I've seen gas station and fast food jobs posted most lately.

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I hope so. To make matters worse I've messed up my UI paperwork it seems and now that is delayed.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: we are all going through a tough time right now with the coronavirus going around and it will make everyone get depressed but don't let it get to you

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I
 am trying, thanks for all your helpful words!
 I am trying to work in the mall now, as I think holidays are going to cause good business. Hope to get something. Maybe I will get a job where I can get a discount!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you should go to places and talk to the hiring manager and see if they are hiring

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I've tried that a few times, but it hasn't worked out that well...it seems like the managers are usually not there. I need a plan/time that I think I'll have more success. So far it's been hard. I'd like to find a job fair, but they are all cancelled from the pandemic.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: yes i can understand they are never there when you go try to talk to them

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Or maybe they are there but they are hiding?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: but all you can do is try right now

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for your support...sometimes I feel like I am the little fish in the big pond.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: sometimes all we need is some support  just keep searching online and going to different places and putting in job applications i am really sure you will get a job soon

situation: nothing i do pleases my boss

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi - how are you today?

dialog.content: hey! an doing better but things at work place gets me real down

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sorry to hear that.  What's going on at work that's making you upset?

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
nothing i do pleases the man
 all he does is complain

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ugh - is he like this with everyone else, too - or do you think it's just directed at you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i think its directed to me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Can you give an example and then maybe we can replay it a different way?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: if am asked to complete a task, i do complete them and make sure no mistake was made, if he will evaluate the task he always sees fault in what i do

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hmm = that is frustrating.  Can you sit with him and go over each section that he finds fault with and ask how he would have done it?

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: maybe talk to his secretary and make an appointment with him.  don't quit the job unless you have another one lined up.

dialog.content: i never tried talking to his secretary  i think that's a good idea

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: she's your first line to getting alone time with him!  she'll get you an appointment for sure!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i try doing just that, i hope it works

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you're welcome.  make sure you bring a list of questions, too!  good luck! Enjoy the rest of the weekend.

situation: I learned that my friend's husband was shot and killed over a drug deal. He was high at the time. My daughter carpools with his daughter every day and he was often the driver. Was he high when he was driving her around? I don't know how to act around my friend. Did she know?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there, how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am fine thank you. How are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am very well. Do you have any problems you'd like to discuss with me?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes. I am having trouble figuring out how to handel a situation with a friend.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah, can you give me a few more details? I am sure I will be able to help/

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Her husband was justshot and kiled over a drug deal.  He was high on drugs at the time.  My dayghter and his daughter are good friends and we carpool with them every day. He was often the driver not my friend.  Was he high when he was driving my daughter arround? Did my friend know he was on drugs?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow that is a tough one - I can see why you would be concerned about it. At least, though, you can be assured that she is safe and is never going to come to any harm as a result of his driving now

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Agreed but it is hard to not be suspicious that the mom knew something about hi actions. Yet I feel horrible that she lost her husband.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand but right now is probably not the best time to confront her about it :(

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I know. I am trying to be supportive and help her with her kids but a part of me is still anxious that she may have played me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: People can hold values that to other people seem strange. She might not have considered it wrong to be driving on drugs, even though it is.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Very true. I really never even considered that!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That would not make it acceptable though!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, absolutely not!  But I never looked at it that way.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: At least you can feel relieved that your daughter is safe now :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes. I am very relieved. He was recently in a car accident before he died. Luckily no one was hurt and the girls were not in the car! He totaled his brand new truck though.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think the best you can do right now is just be there for your friend when she wants to talk.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Definitely trying to be!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is she able to support herself financially?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes she received payouts from several large life insurance policies so they are safe financially. Just a mess emotionally.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Maybe it can be a good opportunity for her to change her lifestyle and get off the drugs  On that note, I hope I have been able to give you some reassurance and thanks for talking!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you so much! You were very helpful!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you're welcome :) (you need to quit your end; I can't from this end :))  if you just press quit and answer the questions ;)

situation: i'm doing a bad job at my job and there are rumors i'll be fired soon. i am a newscaster at a small radio station. basically this job is too much for me.  i feel tremendous pressure.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How can I help you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i think i'm about to get fired from my job. i feel tremendous pressure. the uncertainty is kiling me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm so sorry to hear that you are about to lose your job.  That sounds very stressful.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i'm not certain. i hear rumors.  it would have been nice if my boss spoke to me directly instead of blabbing about this behind my back.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds very difficult that things are not openly discussed. It sounds like you want to be proactive which is good though.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: it's awful.  this job is too hard for me.  getting fired would put me in a big financial hole.  my head is spinning.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you thought at all about looking for a new job that might not be so stressful?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i am about to start doing that but it's tricky. i'm a radio announcer and if your boss finds our you're looking for another job he can use that as a reason to fire you.  there are ways to get around this but it's complicated. small time radio is a difficult mess.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there anyone you can contact who has been in a similar situation to help you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i wish there was someone like that.  i've talked to people in the past about this type of situation but i wasn't the one under the gun.  but your advice is good and i'll try and think of someone who might be able to help me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great that you are so willing to try different options to move forward.  Perhaps a family member may help?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i haven't told the specifics of this mess to my parents; i dont want to burden them with this.  they worry about me enough as it is.  my sisters wouldn't be able to help me.  they both have seirous emotional issues and are both in very bad marriages and have plenty of their own problems.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Just from chatting with you, I can see why you are anxious.  Have you tried any deep breathing techniques.  They are often helpful when dealing with stress.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that's a good idea.  i think i'll try that. and praying.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Praying is also an excellent idea.  My friends and I prayed all day long for each other during a difficult exam that I previously had failed and had to retake.  That day everyone passed the exam.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: praying is a wonderful thing that i need to do more often.  i'm glad you passed the exam.  i had a lot of pressure when i went to college as well. it seems most of my 24 years alive i've had one sort of probem or another but i always remember that i have plenty of things to be thankful for too. life can be rough.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
My grandmother always encouraged me by saying there is always someone who has it rougher.  
 Things can be really hard but you can do it.  You are working hard and trying so hard.

situation: I have had a very difficult time during this holiday season. I lost my job, and have experiences problems with my significant other. I sleep more than normal, and have been drinking heavily.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Good morning

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Merry Christmas

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Good morning Merry Christmas How may I help you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I'm just struggling real bad with depression lately.
 I feel like I'm not any good, but the other part of my mind says that just isn't true.
 Despite having those conflicting thoughts....I can't get going.
 I've been in a downward spiral....IDK how to escape it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am so sorry i know how you are feeling just know everything is going to be okay what has been making your depression start?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I mean things do typically work themselves out, they always do....but I just can't get out of this rut I've been in.

 Well I lost my job because my GF had texted me saying she didn't want to be with me anymore....and I just couldn't handle it.
 Then I just started going downhill from there.....have had difficulty finding another job, or trying to replace lost income.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you looked into seeking therapy that will sometimes help with your depression

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I have an appointment scheduled with a Doctor on Tuesday actually....
 I made a call the other day....which was difficult in and of itself

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is good. Have you been looking for another job putting in applications everywhere?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I have....I've had difficulty trying to receive a call back though.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know it is hard right now finding a job with this pandemic going on and it will take time but i know you will find a job soon

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: which has been frustrating in and of itself. I know it's all mental, but I just can't break through.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You have to remain positive and keep your head and tell your self everything will be okay I can understand how you are feeling and you are not alone

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I really appreciate you listening, and understandig
 it's tremendously helpful

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are welcome. Everything will be okay just know that.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You're right...I do know that, I just need to truly believe it. Every day is a new day for opportunity and to be better then yesterday.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Right and you have to take control of those negative thoughts and replace them with positive thoughts

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I could do a better job of reframing situations and looking at everything as 'glass half full' instead of empty

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are right you have to learn to control your feelings towards a situation

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do tend to overreact or just go overboard pretty easily.....I agree with you there.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Don't let depression take full control of you then it will be all bad

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's true....I will start doing a better job at looking at the brighter side of things....it's not as bad as I make it seem I guess.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: once you learn how to have control over your feelings over situations then it will be pretty easy to maintain

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think seeing the doctor will help a ton too. you've been extremely helpful

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes seeing a doctor would really be helpful for you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for spending 15 minutes talking to me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No problem. have a Merry Christmas and cheer up please :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will, you've been a huge help :D

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am glad I helped you today. Take Care.

situation: worried about losing my job

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am very worried about losing my job

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello - How are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Anxious. I think my boss wants to fire me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am so sorry you are worried about losing your job. I have been in your shoes before.  Why do you think your boss wants to fire you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Thank you. I think if I am late again, I will be let go.

 I have a sick child and my boss does not understand
 How can I explain that I may be late to the office, but that I am a valuable employee?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you explained to him your reasoning? I also once had to take care of my sick grandmother after work and had a tough time explaining it to my boss.  Have you offered to stay later to make up the work?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No. But that is a good idea.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Maybe your boss can compromise with you. It's worth a shot!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's a very competitive office and I don't want to look weak.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You sound like you care about this job and that is a valuable asset in a an employee.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do. I hope I can explain the situation clearly and offer to make up time by staying late.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand. Is there a coworker or mentor that maybe you can talk to?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will reach out to HR.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's a great idea!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you. You have been very helpful and uplifting.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Also review your employee handbook and see if there are things you can do to negotiate with HR or your boss in regards to your hours.  Always best to come prepared :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My anxiety is disappearing with the suggestions you have offered.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great! I am happy I could help you during such a difficult time. How long have you been at the job?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have only been here for 6 months. I think being new is contributing to the anxiety I am feeling.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand. Being new at a job can have its difficulties.  From speaking with you, it seems like you are caring and hardworking.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for your encouragement.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hope your boss can understand you are facing a difficult time. If he has loved ones or family of his own, he surely will.  Is there anything else I can help you with today?

situation: I broke up with my partner last month and I'm in a funk

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello. How are you tonight?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm hanging in there. I'm in a funk.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sorry to hear things aren't going to well. What's the problem in a nutshell?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I split with my girlfriend after 5 years together last month/

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Breakups can be hard, I can understand your frustration. Five years is a long time to have a relationship and then end.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I caught her cheating on me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sorry to hear that. That's hard to forgive and move on from that.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm trying to just move on but I feel so insulted. I'm mad then sad back and forth, over and over.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I find that when I have a very painful situation, I try to only thing about it for a certain amount of time. Then I make a list of things to do so I can be occupied to not think about it.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, but my whole life was upended. My living situation, my friend circle is broken up over this. I don't know how to move on. Any suggestions?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Getting over a breakup will take time? Have you ever heard of DABDA? Denial (I can't believe this happened!) Anger (we all know that) Bargaining (If only I had done that, or if only - trying to make it FEEL better by imagining other scenarios - doesn't really work) Depression - sadness, then Acceptance -- we go in and out of these emotions and sometimes it can make us feel crazy, but all of them are normal in varying degrees and we don't know when they will happen.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, but those sound like the typical stages of grief. I guess I'm in anger. I have no interest in barbagining.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I would make a list of things you have always wanted to do and then just BRAINSTORM any idea, no idea is too out there -- learn to ice skate, learn to play the ukulele, sign up for dance lessons, join the gym, go to a singles group at church, visit the elderly, volunteer somewhere.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think I need to focus on exercise. Just get my body and mind healthy and full of energy. What do you think?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I also find it helpful not only to limit how much I dwell on my pain, but make sure I reach out to help someone else. I will tell you, that is the secret to happiness. Maybe help a neighbor with groceries or hanging lights. Old people (me) love help, bake something or call your aunt or uncle.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: True, but it's hard not to dwell. It's not like I have a choice. It overcomes me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I agree, get healthy and try to help others. One thing about feelings is that if you change your mind, you can change your feelings, it might happen slowly, but if you put your mind off of it, it will help. Not saying NEVER feel the emotions, but feelings are the tail of the dog, not the dog itself, You do not have to be ruled by them. You don not have to FEEL like going for a walk to go for a walk.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's hard not to just drown myself in booze and cannabis.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: A healthy balance of self care, and other centered behavior and know this will take time. I just takes time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Time heals all wounds for sure

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand, but those are choices that really, long term, are not for your benefit. Her cheating was a reflection on HER bad character, not yours. You have to love yourself and try to fight this.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Are you Ai or a human mturker?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm a human. I live in KY, retired counselor! hhahaha I prayed for someone to encourage.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: OH cool. Well your prayer came true. I'm, also a human....from Georgia

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm from the city, so there isn't much to do on a farm in the middle of cold weather. I have been through a lot of breakups, so I know what works, and yes, I was cheated on, devastating. But you can overcome this. Make that list and do NOT let your feelings rule your life. You can recover and find someone worthy of your love and care.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks so much!!! I really appreciate it! :)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: The WORST breakup came a year before I met my husband. in retrospect, it was the best thing, listen,you could have married this girl, count your blessings. cheating is just terribly destructive. And you are welcome. Take care of yourself. Write the list of goals and fun things to do, choose one every day.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You are very kind! All the best!! Take care!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Bye now! God bless!

situation: My girlfriend broke up with me recently. Really not feeling it right now, especially since I could've dedicated more time to her after exams but she got fed up. I guess I'm just alone for Christmas.

hello how are 
 you

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker

ive been smoking a lot more because of this incident, what else can I do to cope?
 and vaping

situation: I am very anxious about loved ones with COVID right now and finances this close to the Holidays

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am having a very anxious day today

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello! I am here to help, could you please tell me what the issue is?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My mother in law is sick with COVID and my husband, when he contracted COVID from her, got terminated from his job.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I can see why you feel anxious, I would be too given the situation.
 When did your mother in law got sick with COVID?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah, it's just really stressful and I am over anxious about it.
 Right before Thanksgiving, she is elderly and has cancer, she is finally feeling better though.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Cancer is already a hard enough disease to fight against. My aunt is a cancer survivor. I am sure your mother can make it too.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: She is a fighter.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: From my knowledge, people usually recover from COVID after 2 to 3 weeks, you might consider having a doctor see your mother if she is still struggling.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, she went again the other day. She is doing quite a bit better. He said because of underlying conditions it may take her longer, but she is doing great. That was encouraging.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
There are substantially more people recovering from covid each day despite the number of infected growing exponentially. I am sure that, with the proper care, your mother in law will make it, and so will your husband.
 I am sure he will be able to find a new job soon as new business start to open, even if it's a temporary one.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, I guess you're right. I know you're right. I just haven't felt really in the Christmas spirit this year and I am anxious about everything I can't control.  He did have an interview today too, so that is good news.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Those are some great news indeed. I am sure he will get a call soon : )

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Lol - you made me smile. Thanks. I will believe that too.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am glad I can make your day just a little better, that's what we are here for ^_^

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It really did help. Just talking and saying it out loud (or online) helped. Feeling heard helped.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: And I am glad you are someone easy to talk to! Some of my patients do not argument at all and expect me to end the conversation when I, as a therapist, have to make sure they are good to go! Helping you has been a pleasure!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am thankful for your listening to me. It does make a big difference. I am in a better mood. Thank you!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are very welcome!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks and have a great day!

situation: friends trying to let me down

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hello

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hope you are doing well

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i'm good, what is troubling you this afternoon?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am not happy with my friends. They were trying to let me down

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: if i get you clearly, you mean your friend disappointed you. how?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes. There were a argument between two of my friend while trying to resolve the issue they all started targeting me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i have a hard time with my friends too sometimes

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Then what you do at that time

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i used to give them time to cool off, when they are back to their senses, they will contact me and apologize  what causes the argument in the first place ?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I was trying to stop the fight, I just said bit loud , "Stop  all this  nonsense at my place." Then they all started backfiring me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: oh,jst give them time to reflect on what happen, they will definitely reach out to you, but you also have to make an effort to forgo the situation because they see you raising your voice on them as a sign of command of seniority.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I feel like not talking to them. Without any reason they made such a big mess I feel like they are not my true friends. you are right I should give them time to realize their mistakes

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: exactly, that will also gives you the necessary time to know how to react to their forthcoming apologies

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for all your suggestions. I would try to implement on it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: even if they haven't reach out, that doesn't mean they are not sorry just be patient with them

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks! I would not going to talk to them have a wonderful New Year! Bye

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: yes, give them time  same to you friend

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: bye

situation: My friend questioned our friendship when I did not want to hang out with her during COVID. She blocked my number when I expressed how upset I feel about what she said.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hey! How can I help?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
So I don't know if you can see the topic of my problem like I can see on my screen?
 If not I'll explain

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes please do

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
So I had a long time friend who recently blocked me from everything. The reason was I refused to hang out with her during covid and she questioned our friendship. Something she said was "It's selfish of me to work rather than spend time with a special
 friend."  So I got upset and called her out. And in response she blocked me.
 And I'm more upset at myself because I wanted to tell her more about how horrible of a human being she is in general. 
 But I cannot because she blocked me on everything.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Have you explained to her that you NEED to work, and don't be upset with yourself because you did the right thing
 If your friendship was strong she will surely unblock you Give her time to cool down

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah I did. And she just got more upset and said I was selfish. 
 Like she's the type who would not back down
 and she's lost many friends and romanics partners that way
 So I feel like she's not going to unblock me and get back together. 
 I'm just really upset because I want to call her off, not because I lost a friendship. 
 Because screw her : ) I put up with her too much.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are not the one who is selfish here as you have done the right thing, and she will hopefully see that

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Should I go to her house and personally tell her how I feel?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If she doesn't then you know she wasn't worth your time in the first place And no because this will make her more angry If she is aware of how you feel, she will come back in due time

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I guess I'm the type of person who has to tell people how I feel, otherwise I won't find peace. 
 Do you have any other suggestions how I can get passed this?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: But for now, you should find other ways to make peace with yourself, for example, hanging out with other friends and that may take your mind of it and if that isn't an option simply keeping yourself busy may help

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: But I can't really hang out with anyone because of COVID. That's how this whole situation started : )

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Maybe spend more time with work, or keeping yourself occupied with other things And she will come back if she really cared about your relationship in the first place

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yeah, work can keep my mind off all this.  And that's true. 
 Thank you for listening to my ranting.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Your welcome, there is always someone out there for you :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Aw thank you! You have a good one.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too!

situation: Friends keep calling my face ugly

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello. Is anyone there

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello. How are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm doing good. I need some advice

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What can I help you with?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Lately, my friends started to make jokes about my face being ugly. They even called my face as flat as a pan! What should I do? I see them everyday on the bus

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you tried confronting them and asking why they are being bullies?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I did and they respond by saying that they are just joking and that I'm handsome. But it's starting to get on my nerves because it's all they make fun about every time
 they see me!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sure you are very handsome! They could just be jealous.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Haha I would hope so
 Still though, it makes me want to avoid them when I go to school on the bus
 What do you think I should do then?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I understand, school was tough for me too.
 I suggest you tell them to knock it off or you can sit somewhere away from the bus.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Should I just beat them up with my backpack

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No, beating them up will only get you in trouble and that won't help.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: There's another bus route. Should I just take that instead

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think that could be a good idea if it's not too far out of your way.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think I will. Their comments make me very insecure

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You could also tell a teacher what is happening. They will help you out. I bet you are handsome! Don't let it get to you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
The teacher would probably make fun of me!

 Thanks it's hard to look in the mirror now

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Try to tell yourself you are good looking and deserve good things to happen every morning! Look in that mirror and tell yourself good things.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I'll try to be positive for once.
 Do you believe working out will help

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Working out will help a lot! When I was feeling down on myself I decided to go to the gym three times a week. It was a great confidence boost.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Awesome. I will start to take care of myself and not worry about others then.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds like a fantastic idea.

situation: my team lead give me a assignment during holiday but i can't do that

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello! How are you feeling today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i am feel worsen

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's never good. I know how it can be to feel down. What's got you feeling that way?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i can't done by project , my team lead will definetly scold me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It seems like you're worried about your abilities as well as your team leader potentially being disappointed with your performance. Am I understanding that correctly?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes , you prediction was so good

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's lovely to hear. Have you tried discussing your doubts with your supervisor? There are times where I've felt overwhelmed, but maybe they could provide some additional guidance or someone to help assist you with the project.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yaa, but this is holiday the supervisor does not take my calls

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there an impending deadline for the project you're worried about meeting? Maybe you could contact them through email or another form of outreach.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes, after the holiday i most submitmy project

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand how stressful that must be, with the business of the holiday season on top of a looming deadline. Is there anyone else you can potentially contact to discuss changing the deadline?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: maybe my team mate will help me , i can communicate them to overcome this

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds like a wonderful idea! That's what a teammate is there for after all.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yaa , you are right

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sure they'd love to help. Is there anything else on your mind today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: nothing

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there anything else you'd like to discuss today?

dialog.speaker: seeker

situation: I am worried about a friend. Since lockdown they have not been in contact and whenever I try to get in touch with them they rarely respond.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there, how can I help you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My friend has been quiet and non responsive for some time and I'm starting to worry about them. This is a pretty recent thing, ever since lockdown.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is this a close friend of yours that you would normally speak to every day or see on a regular basis?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes that's correct, however with lockdown I haven't seen any friends for a long time face to face.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ok so you have are close friend, who you used to engage with regularly and now you are worried because when you try and contact them, they aren't responding.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes that's the issue. I think lockdown is affecting people in many ways and I want to make sure they are OK

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can tell that you feel a lot for your friend and it shows that you care about lot about them.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I do. Now what should I do about them as other friends in our peer group are also concerned?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ok, I understand. Have you tried reaching out to their family members as they may have a loser handle on how this person is feeling at the moment?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I actually haven't. I'm not that close to their family to be honest so I'm 
not quite sure how to do that?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You could try reaching out to friends on Facebook or social media, you can find contact details for them that way and perhaps send them a direct message. You are a concerned friend which means they probably wouldn't mind if you did get in touch.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I didn't think about contacting family that way. That's a good suggestion.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I had a friend once that was acting out of character. They weren't quiet but I could tell them here was something different in the way they were acting etc. It turns out that they had started a new relationship with another friend of mine and didn't want anyone else to know. In reality everything was fine. There was an explainable reason behind the behaviour and it was nothing bad in the end.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Perhaps I am worried about nothing and your experience does make sense. I may be reading too much into these things but you read about people's mental health being affected by COVID isolation these days.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is true. Mental health issues have increased  significantly since lockdowns began As they are not responding to your attempts to get in touch, you could think has anything else changed for them other than lockdown, for example are they busier at work? have childcare commitments? Life is complicated, they may not have the time for legitimate reasons.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is true. I think they are probably very busy at the moment but as they are typically so outgoing it did strike me as odd. What else would you suggest?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You mentioned that other friends were also concerned about them, have you tried talking to them about setting up a video call with a group. This might appeal to your friend and they may see this as a fun activity to get involved in.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think I'll give that a try. Maybe they don't have time but a fun relaxing group chat might be the way forward. Thanks for this suggestion. I'll give this a go.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No problem happy to help.

situation: Everyone breaks up with me. They all think I'm weird, and I always screw myself.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi, I'm Anna

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there, how is life treating you right now?  I am Jo, it is nice to meet you! Is there anything you'd like to talk about today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Going through a break-up. My 3rd one this year.... Nice to meet you too
 It suckssssss

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Gosh you've been busy :). I am sorry to hear that though - I know how much it hurts when you split with someone, especially if it was not consensual.  It's a lousy time of year for it too, isn't it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I feel like a piece of crap, and childish at that. I can't maintain any of my relationships. I think I'm going to be alone for the rest of my life.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is a defeatist way to talk! Just because a couple of relationships fail does not mean that you will never be able to find happiness.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, if past experience has any powers of prediction. I get super clingy, and do this push and pull thingy, where I break up and call them names, and then become so embarrassed it's hard to even bring it up. my last boyfriend said he thought i had borderline disorder

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have been in that position too. I hate it when I realise that I am not being rational but I can't seem to stop myself sometimes and this has sometimes let to problems with my inter personal relationships

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yea, that definitely seems to be part of my problem. I really thought he was different, too. And when he broke up with me, I tried to get him to spend the night with me in a hotel, and I felt so pathetic because I was practically begging for sex
 I think I am an addict.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hear you on that one too! It's horrible to feel that you are losing your self respect but I suspect that we've all done it at some point. Have you considered getting some counselling?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Sigh.. yes. I have seen several. I also take meds from a psychiatrist.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hear  you saying that they are not really effective, is that true?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Well... one told me that I should be put down like a dog to my face. 
 so, yea

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay .. I hope you reported him!!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I didn't. I was younger then. who knows. not gonna lie. maybe they're right. Sometimes I think I'm a psycho. My last therapist was always doing deep breathing exercises. It didn't help.
 I also have a lot of anxiety. A LOT!
 I even got an emotiona support dog

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand that as well .. I am on medication for it. A dog is a wonderful thing to have! Do you feel that it has helped you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yea, he's my best friend. At least I have one boy who has to stick around. He's on a tight leash

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Dogs are very very loyal. They never cheat on you or walk out on you!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: So trueee :) I just wish I wasn't such a freak.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think you are probably not anywhere near as bad as you think you are you know :). Anyway I wish you all the very best for the New Year and hope that things pick up for you soon!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Alright, bud. Thanks. You, too

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: take care!  (you need to quit first and take the survey .. I can't :))

situation: My youngest sister is battling with heroin addiction, and I am raising her two children.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello, thanks for offering to help.  My sister is battling with heroin addiction, and I am raising her children.  It's quite a bit of pressure and responsibility.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi! Of course. I am happy to help.  Oh wow, that is really stressful. You are such a godo person for taking on her children. You should feel really good about yourself.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: She has been in and out of rehab, and I see no end to her problems, other than a tragic one.  Thankfully, the children are adjusting to it, probably because they are happy just to be in a stable environment.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you raising them alone?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I have a romantic partner who helps.  I also have two of my own relatively young children.
 So, four in total.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's a lot of children! It's great that you have a partner who can help you.  A dear friend of mine is in rehab and had to give up her children, it is really awful to watch. It makes me feel so helpless, so i understand how you msut be feeling.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Thanks for being sympathetic and for the words of encouragement.  Yes, needless to say, Child Protective Services were involved, and I was granted Foster Parent status.  I'm sure your friend went down that road, sadly.
 Have you ever seen the video by the artist known as NF called "How Could You Leave Us?"  I'm afraid that's what's next for these kids.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, she did. Her children seem very lucky to have you. Do they get along with your children? I haven't seen that, waht happens in it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
They all get along famously.   You can find that video on YouTube.  It's a true story of how his mother died from heroin addiction.  It's a powerful song.

 Hopefully, you never have to experience it.  Heroin (opioids) just grabs hold of people and doesn't let go.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's so awful. the only thing you can do is show the kids love and make them feel cared for.  Addiction is a truly awful disease, people don't appreciate the toll it takes on families.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you.  One piece of good news.  Her son went from failing most of his classes to making Honor Roll this trimester - that's what his advisor tells me.   You've been really helpful and considerate.  Chatting with you certainly made me feel better.  I think we can end the support session at this juncture if you've met your session requirements.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's so good!!! I hope i was helpful!! have a great day and continue being such a good support for the kids. I am very impressed.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks so, much.  Have a great holiday and we'll try to do the same.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too. Enjoy the holidays!!!  Hopefully, you all get to spend so much time togehter.

situation: My company just laid off 5%. I fear I will be next. Anxiety is at all time highs.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi, how's your day going?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, my day is goin well.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Good to hear, not so much for me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that, what is going on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am stressed about losing my job due to this pandemic.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: To be sure I understand correctly, you are worried you are going to lose your job because of COVID-19.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, that's correct.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I completely understand this feeling as when the pandemic began I was also extremely worried about my job security as well.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
My place just when into a lock down. I feel like small business will be crushed. 
 Job stability is a big concern for me at the moment.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can definitely tell through chatting with you that you are extremely anxious about the situation.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, indeed I am. If I were to get fired. Finding another job in this crisis would be rough.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What industry do you work in?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Accounting. Low entry, bookkeeper.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you talked to your manager about the situation?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, my manager is very strict. I would not want to anger him. Many people felt his wraith already due to this pandemic.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I understand your anxiety due to this, but I know you can make it through this situation!
 Have you talked to friends or family members about this? I found that when I was going through my anxiety, talking to my family was very useful.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, but I've been taking medication for my anxiety.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have been on anxiety medicine before and it definitely helped me as well.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Good to hear, hopefully it will help me. It's only be a week since I start taking it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You can definitely get through this!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for the positive vibes.

situation: I recently lost my job and I am worried that i will not able to pay my bills. This situation is making me anger.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Hi, I can't see the upper box that you can see, so what brings you 
to seek support?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: can you see it now?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I'm sorry, I cannot. Sadly, the program is bad.
 You will have to restate your concerns.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I recently lost my job  I am very upset about my economic situation

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm so sorry. I'm sure that would be very upsetting, and very concerning for your future. That sounds awful!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I am worried that I will not  be able to pay my bills,  I am looking for a job but because of the pandemic is hard to get one any advice

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay, so it sounds like you cannot pay the bills, you are looking for a job, and the economic situation is tough right now due to the pandemic. This is your main concern for the conversation, then? To discuss how to secure another job?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes It is my main concern

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you consulted with a job center, a life coach, or any other resource such as online websites? These may be useful.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have looked on websites. They offer with job consultants  but there is a lot of volume of calls with the same problem

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sure you will be able to get a new job if you have the commitment. Maybe you can tell me a little more about what type of job you are looking for?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am looking for a customer service type of job where I can work from home if is possible

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay, that sounds like a great start. Many jobs have moved to being remote, so I can see that as a great alternative. I know there are many covid tracing jobs by phone that pay well. Have you searched for those on any online websites? Would that interest you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I had  search I am still waiting for a reply. I really interests me since I have some customer service experience.  I hope they call me soon.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow, that is terrific! I know those are in high-demand right now, so I wouldn't be surprised if they did call you back. Your experience will definitely make you more marketable as well.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes , thanks for your help and advice

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you had anyone look at your resume to see if improvements could be made while you wait?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I have someone check my resume and update it .

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Okay, that's great. Today, many resumes are not screened by humans, but by computers, analyzing for key words, first.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes, thanks a lot for your time

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are welcome. I hope you don't give up, and stay optimistic! It sounds like you have a great start.

situation: COVID, job pressure, stress with partner, sharing custody of my son, recuperating from cancer treatment

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello, how are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Im am fine, How's things today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Stressful, but I have to deal with it. I can't afford to not be present.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand that many people now in these days are in a position where he or she needs to be at a location and that is stressfull

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
So i guess the point of this is for me to talk about what it is that I'm facing?
 I have to have a brave face for everyone because I'm in charge of young people. I have to be strong for them, but it leaves me feeling exhausted

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Being a father of two girls makes my skin cringe. Young people can be very challenging. Tell me more about this?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I teach special needs students, and they definitely make me happy. It's a very, very rewarding job. However, with political division and COVID, it's hard to reach this population. Parents are not supportive. I feel like I'm being stretched so thin.
 I'm also a parent, and my son is doing virtual learning, so he's just as isolated.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I remember a time where learning crisis management that taking a deep breath and meditating in the positive work that you are doing can open up your mind to suggestions from your inner self. Have you ever thought that you superb skills can transmit in your home as well?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Getting to that mindsight is challenging when the overwhelming feelings of 'failure' loom so heavily. I was seeing a therapist and making great strides, then he informed me that he was moving to a private practice. I cannot afford his sliding scale, and I feel let down. It's just that when one thing starts to go wrong, other things follow. Most of the time I see myself as a positive person that can work through challenging situations, but like you said it's a tough time for everyone

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So now that you understand that you have a awesome skill and that is being positive, build on this. What building blocks can you write down that has helped you pushed through the fire and keep it some where close to you to always give you piece of mind and reinforce your habits of pushing through the fire.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well, I am not one for journaling or anything. I try to stay active. I am about 6 months into remission from stage 4 HL (cancer) and it's been quite the emotional journey there. With that said, the mental and physical overhaul left quite a dent in my overall feelings about myself. I'm just now able to run again and in the past week I ran 2 5k's. To me, this was monumental and made me feel great. I'm also learning how to not overextend myself. I just keep hoping for continued improvement in all areas. The reinforcement is just that I can get through this, these feelings are only temporary.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I totally understand that you are not for writing things down to keep. Yet, you have gone through a alot of events in your life where other would have given up. The key is transmitting your successes regardless of the situations. COVID is not going anywhere and as a person who has beaten stage 4 cancer, it is scary to be living in these times. You are doing monumental things, never forget that because your feelings and stamina for living will bind everything together. Politics, is the dirtiest and no government can solve  and will solve the problems in the world.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thank you for listening. It helps to sometimes just get it out there. I have to go coach now (virtually). Have a great evening.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
You too as well
 anytime

situation: I lost my job 4 month ago and I am running out of money.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I was looking for some assistance. I lost my job 4 month ago

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Losing your job is one of the biggest stressors in life, how have you been handling it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well first few month was kind off alright, Was looking for a job in different places without success. And now I am really depressed and I feel so useless.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I know what it's like to lose a job, and when it's hard to find a new one it can really make you feel low. What activities have you been doing in addition to seeking new employment?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Nice that you understand me, I was trying job websites alongside with jobcenter in town centre. I have jobseeker allowance but it will not cover the rent

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am living in a high-rent city and the only way I was able to keep my apartment was to find a roommate. Are there ways you can get help from friends or family until a new job opens up?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am renting just a room at the moment, Before I had a girlfriend so we had to split the bill. After she left I had to take all responsibility just on myself to pay bills. I have few friends but don't think they could lend me a lot of money anyway

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: One possible solution, if it's possible for you, could be to sub-lease your room to someone else and stay with friends or family. Even if they can't loan you money, they may be able to let you stay with them for a week while you make money renting your room as an airbnb.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh AirBnB might be a solution actually. I will speak to my cousin if I could stay couple of nights at his place.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What sort of employment are you looking for? Perhaps we can brainstorm some places you can go to make connections for potential work.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I was working in IKEA warehouse before I got redundant

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Working in a warehouse shows you have great skills for several other types of jobs. Have you checked small independent-contractor sites like TaskRabbit or Lyft to make extra funds while you search for a new permanent job?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I even haven't heard about those. I am really useless sometimes... What kind of jobs I can find there do you know?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well, if you didn't know about them, that's not your fault. It's possible that you could find a new job opportunity while working on smaller tasks or by volunteering for a local organization. You could meet new folks who could provide you with new connections for employment!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I Will definitely try that. You are really helpful. Have you been doing any jobs through those websites?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes I am able to make a little spending money by doing small tasks like delivering real estate papers, or delivery take-out food. You can google a list of sites like RentSons.com where you can sign up to do an assortment of tasks, and your warehouse skills will come in handy!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's cool, I feel now a lot better. Maybe I will be able to find even a girlfriend somewhere on those jobs. I feel lonely after my girlfriend left me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: We all feel lonely when life gets hard, but getting yourself out there to do tasks will help you feel useful AND you could meet your future wife, you never know! I wish you the absolute best!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you very much for you help. I learned something new today and feel much better

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome. I think you'll do great.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Have a lovely
 afternoon!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too!

situation: I decided to separate from my husband after finding myself incredibly unhappy.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello my name is Christina

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello Christina

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What's your name?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Shay, thank you for asking

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi Shay. You are welcome.  Anything on your mind today? I'm here if you would like to talk about anything.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am feeling depressed. I feel lonely and unimportant to those around me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So you are feeling depressed and lonely and you feel unimportant?  I am sorry to hear that you are feeling this way. I have felt the same before and I know it can be difficult to change those feelings.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yes
 I just want to get out of this slump. I hate that im sad all the time

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand that.  If I may can I ask you if you live with anyone?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: i live by myself now

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ok well that should be something that you can take pride in.  Do you have contact with your friends and family?  I have found that for me calling my mom or chatting with my friends that truly know me makes me feel more connected to others.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I talk to my mom often, but i was isolated from most of my friends for years so im trying to meet new ones.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great that you speak to your mom.  I know it can be hard to open yourself up and meet new friends but I think you can do it.  Are there maybe some people at your job that you would consider making friends with?  I think that would be a great place to start because you would already have something in common.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do have friends from my job, but i currently commute 1.5 hours for work which makes it hard. I feel that with time i may be more open to more friends, but right now i just want those that already know my back story for comfort.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: OK I see.  So for now you would like to be able to get comfort from the people that already know you and your story.  One suggestion that I have would be to really try to reach out and contact those people.  I know that with work and life in general it is easy to lose touch.  Not that you stop caring for a person but things just get hetic from time to time.

situation: MY Son-in-Law is treating our daughter and Grandaughter indifferent, gaslighting and treats the dog better.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Hi and good afternoon
 Thank you for your time

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, and good afternoon yourself. How are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Doing pretty good. Struggling a little

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What is making you struggle?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My situation involves our Daughter and Son-in-Law. He is not treating her well, rude, gaslighting and treating the dog better. He is also not very nice to our grandaughter This is confidential, Right?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, of course it is :). So we are talking about your daughter and her husband and their child together, right?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, He is a nice guy to others. At home he is so different.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand, people can often show different sides depending on who is around. Do you have any reason to think your daughter or granddaughter are in physical danger?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, they are safe. He's, like I said, a nice guy. Our Daughter, we are very close and she would tell us.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great news! It sounds as though you are just concerned about how she is sometimes being treat. Have you had any conversation with your daughter about their relationship?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes, numerous. Like I said we are close to our daughter. She wants to stay together and talks with him. 
 He stays for whatever reason. I wish he would talk to me, to us. We have offered.
 He knows that we care about him.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: And that is so important and good to know there is trust from his side, to me it sounds as though that could be an olive branch for you to try and establish a conversation with both of them. An open and positive communication is always the first step in resolving anything.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: We have tried this before when we visit and he appears receptive. Treats he well and then when we are gone. He falls back into he behaviour.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I recently went though a similar exercise with a friend and her boyfriend. We sat down in a room and spoke about our feelings. We had ground rules first and everyone knew they could talk without being judged or escalating. Maybe you could suggest follow up conversations, re-cap about what was spoken about previously and they can both evaluate how they have progressed since then?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thats sounds like a good idea. We have talked with them individually. Now the next step. We shall see. Yeah consistency leads to accountability. I like the idea. Thank you.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I believe you have both their interests in mind. You sound like a positive person and you are just concerned for all 3 people involved.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: For sure, she is our daughter and our granddaughter needs her daddy.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hope they will be well receptive of your suggestion for a follow up.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you. I have some good ideas after chatting with you.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No worries, I'm grateful for your time as well. If there is nothing else, I hope it all goes well and you have a wonderful weekend :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You, too. I appreciate your time, listening ear and advice. Take care and God Bless.

situation: I have found out that my friends have been socialising behind my back without me. This makes me very sad.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi there, are you free to talk? I am feeling rather sad.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there. Yes, What is making you sad?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I feel that my friends do not like me very much any more. Apparently they have been engaging in group activities without me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand how frustrating that can be to not be part of the group activities.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Do you think that I am over-reacting? I do not want to sound over sensitive but this has been happening for a while.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you talked with your friends on how you feel? When was the last time you all connected?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: We went out for coffee the other day and I thought about saying something cos I know they went to a club without me last weekend but I couldn't bring myself to in the end.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand it is tough to bring up what is bothering you, especially after getting to hang out with all your friend over coffee. I'm sure this was not intentional with not inviting you to the club.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Sometimes in the past I have turned down invitations to go out clubbing, admittedly. Could it be that they just did not think that I wanted to go?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: From what I have experienced personally, I am not a fan of clubs and didnt want to go with my friends. After turning them down a few times, they stopped inviting as well. I think they thought that was not my thing.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: The thing is though that this is not the only thing - they have been to rock concerts without me too. I just feel I am no longer a part of the group.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I feel how this is hurting you. The best thing would be to talk to them about feeling disinvited to group activities. Let them know you are still free and really want to spend some time together. Do you think you can open up to them on whats bothering you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't know. I don't want to come across as just being paranoid or whiny. Sometimes I think it would be better to just forget them and not have any friends.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like this situation is really bothering you. Its hard and overwhelming to feel isolated. The bonds of friendship are worth the confrontation. If they don't know what is bothering you, it will be harder to mend the relationship.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It is making me really depressed and is badly damaging my sense of self worth. I feel as though I am not worth anyone's time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are worth every precious second of time and should not let this diminish your self worth. Find common ground, an activity you all enjoy and invite them to hang with you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you that is helpful. What if I reach out and they all snub me though?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: We all have busy hectic lives at time. True friends make time to be with one another. This world is full of beautiful people to bond with. Keeping a closed circle of friends is a blessing.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It's not really so much that my life is hectic. On the contrary, sometimes it feels as though it is rather empty and not having the support of friends does not help with that.  Anyway I do appreciate your help - you have given me things to think about.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hear how this is making you sad not feeling supported in the way that you need to be. Know that you are worthy and will find a way to connect with them. I hope this has helped.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It has .. thank you very much and enjoy your day!

situation: Really dislike my job in this current environment and would like to try online sales
