dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have been in this same position! Sometimes you have to take a leap, even though you are not sure what might happen. So far everything has worked out fine for me.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's true. I just don't want to lose the benefits that I am currently entitled to at my current bad job

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It is a tricky situation, to be sure. Have you talked to anyone else about it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes my wife, and she is supportive either way so that's not a problem

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am glad to hear that you have someone supportive in your life. What is your current line of work?

dialog.content: e-commerce manager for walmart stores inc

dialog.speaker: supporter

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: looking to work exclusively at home in sales which is more my specialization, so yes to both questions

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Online work can be very lucrative. I have been doing it for years personally. It is nice to be your own boss and choose your own hours.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I currently do day trading on the side and make great money at it but want something stable to challenge me

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It is true that stability is important. Do I hear you say that you are frightened of the insecurity of working from home?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: more so the lack of benefits I would lose, especially healthcare

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah .. I am in the UK so we have socialised medicine :). Would you feel more comfortable if you lived in a country like that?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: lol definitely! Currently I pay $750 monthly if I purchase healthcare on my own but only $163 monthly through my work

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Goodness .. I do not have to pay anything. Also our prescriptions are fixed at about ten pounds each.  I think maybe your job dilemma would be easier if you moved to a different country :D

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I would lose about $3000 monthly leaving my job

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You might be able to make that much from home though. And what's life if you don't take the odd risk?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: still there?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes I am :)  Is there anything else that you'd like to talk about ?  I hope I have been able to be of some help to you :)

situation: I don't know what to do anymore, everything in my life is going downward.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm good thank you, how about you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am okay thanks. Is there anything you'd like to talk to me about right now?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I don't know what to do anymore, I'm just lazy on everything. I don't have any motivation anymore

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds to me like you are suffering from depression. That sounds scary but it really is common. Would you consider professional help?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't know, I don't even talk to my family anymore, I'm just at my room watching and playing all the time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That definitely does sound like depression to me. I have been there myself and I promise you that there is help available.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I do still to my friends though through voice call, that's the only thing I've been doing lately play games with them
 still talk*

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I guess that the pandemic has not helped at all with things?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yeah the pandemic too affected me but this was going on before the pandemic

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Can't have improved the situation though. Do you know that there are many free counselling services out there that you could utilise?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I know but I don't have the courage to do so, I'm kind of shy, really shy.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hear you - I found it very difficult to go to counselling too but when I did I found it very helpful.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I know I should seek help, but I don't have the guts, what should I do to have the will to do it?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I do not have any easy suggestions. It took me a good deal of time to be brave enough too. But you can ask yourself - can't things only get better? Or even if they don't, you don't really have anything to lose :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I know, I should set aside my pride and seek help, I hope I muster the courage to do it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I hope you can too :). I wish you all the best in the future (can you press quit first - I can't do it from my end :))

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Okay, thank you for your time, I really appreciate it ;)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You too and best wishes!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Have a good day
 likewise

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you just need to press quit and take the survey :)

situation: My friends are always leaving me out of activities.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hello, what is on your mind today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm really sad because I'm feeling left out of my freind's group.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: why is that? because of covid?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
No it's always been like that.
 They will make plans in front of me and not invite me,

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: so, your friend and thier friends make plans in front of you but dont invite you? have you ever just showed up? maybe they assume talking in front of you is like an invite or have you ever asked your friend what is going on?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah. They will also talk about how they did something previously and I wasn't invited. That's not my personality. I think it's rude to show up or assume.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: that has to be frusterating. have you thought about moving on and finding better friends? it sounds like you are a great person and you deserve a lot better than that.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have always been shy and have a very hard time making friends. My hisband is also in the military so we move a lot and it's a constant struggle of trying to fit in with new people. Thank you for saying that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i understand that, i am an army veteran

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah it can be very hard.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: what about having a dinner for some other couples in his unit? have him plan a get together? even drinks and bowling or something

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Unfortunately we can't do that right now because his base is under strict rules for covid. But normally that would be a good idea.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: fair enough. well maybe you can just hang in there till this stupid virus thing is over i heard the fda has approved a vaccine for emergency purposes . i bet military will be some of the first to get it.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah that is true.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i bet once they get the soldiers vaccinated the  rules on get togethers will get less strict!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You're probably right about that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: just hang in there till 2020's over, we don't much longer till 2021, and then its a whole new year!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I hope so!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: the whole world hopes so! lol

situation: I found my mom murdered on the side of her house

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
hello
 I have a real pickle of a problem

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sorry to hear that, can you tell me what it is?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am depressed and all alone

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's a hard situation to be in.  Are you isolating due to the pandemic?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have been isolated for 2 years now ever since my wife decided to sneak out in the middle of the night and run away

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's really rough.  It sounds like you did not get to have any closure whatsoever.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yeah what a peach she was, at least she didnt murder me like my brother did to my mom

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: There's a lot of dark stuff happening in and around your life.  Do you have any things that you like to do on your own that calm you down?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: drinking is the only thing that takes the pain away

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have definitely been there.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: everytime I say it cant get any worse I get a new illness

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It must seem like things are just piling on.  Booze does this crazy thing where we think it's making things easier, but it's really making things 10x harder.  10x harder to process emotions, 10x harder to find the motivation, 10x harder to believe you are worth the effort to keep moving.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: all I have to look forward to is fighting for pennies on mturk all day, who would ever countinue if thats all they had

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think you could give the booze a break for a week, and see if you may see things in a different light.  Zero judgment from me btw, I am also clearly on mturk, and I quit drinking in April after ten years of heavy heavy drinking.  Almost lost my marriage to it.  It's not easy, and I'm not preaching, but I am saying it was the best thing I could do to begin to improve my life and my health, mental and physical.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: wait till everything is taken away from you and your left with nothing and noone and see how you feel then, it might be time for a drink

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm really sorry you're feeling this poorly.  If you could be doing anything at all right now, what would it be?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
riding a motorcyle
 just one more thing I will never get to do again

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh shit I am so terrified of riding motorcycles Why can't you ride motorcycles any more?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: poverty

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah, so you can still physically ride one!  Your life isn't over, you are still breathing, you will ride a motorcycle again.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
i was riding a motorcyle next to my brother about two years before he murdered our mom, got hit by a car and nearly died
 santa is bringing money this year?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Maybe you could rent one

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
or I could just steal one
 I would rather meet a woman that isnt a coocoo bird

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Maybe there are other alternative modes of transportation you could take up

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: well my car is about to die so it will be walking soon

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Perhaps you'll meet someone on the bus?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: going over how horrible everything is for me all day has been a real treat that one really made me laugh

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You didn't seem to be responding to actual solutions so I thought a joke might help :-)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: there arent really any solutions, nothing women like more then a creepy guy talking to you on the bus

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You could be a peer-supporter instead of a seeker, you wouldn't have to talk about your personal stuff, and it's actually been kind of nice to just give people some advice in a vacuum.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I tried but I kept failing the test
 I have got so much going for me, If only the volcano hadnt come and destroyed what little I had left

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's horrible that happened to you, but don't you believe in yourself?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: no not really, nothing to believe in

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You should, you said yourself that you have got so much going for you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that was sarcasm, one of my only friends

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah I see

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: well I tortured you enough thanks for listening

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome, I hope things start getting better soon :-)

situation: I worried if I will be able to complete my degree.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello, how are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am doing great, how about you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am doing good. Can I talk to you about something?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Of course you can, tell me about it :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am worried that I won't be able to complete my degree.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Why so?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I can't get motivated, it doesn't seem worth it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hey I've been there too, but it really helped me meditating and going out with nature to clear my mind of things. You should try it!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What do you meditate about?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Nothing special, just sit in my room in silence. Listen to tutorials on youtube to meditate
 They get me relaxed and motivated for the day :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I feel like I would sit there and worry about things I have not done yet.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's part of meditation! I too worry while meditating, but when i'm listening to the videos and following instructions all those worries go away :) If that doesn't help you, you can go out in nature to clear your mind off things. Being in the wild really motivates me to continue.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Maybe it will calm me down. Does it make it easier to concentrate?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Meditating absolutely does since the whole point is to clear your mind off things and concentrate on the task ahead.
And the wild? that's whole other experience because you either concentrate on things or you get mesmerized by nature :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That sounds amazing, I should give it a try :)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Please do! You can try it with friends or alone. With friends can be a more fun experience while alone can be more spiritual or calming  I have tried both and both are amazing :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I would like to experience that.
 It sounds amazing

situation: I am so stressed and nervous about my hours being cut further due to the pandemic. I tried applying for another job since January and I have not received any responses. I am worried that I will not be able to pay my debts and bills at this rate.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am quite stressed.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well I can definitely understand being stressed at this time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I can't believe that my hours were cut again at my current job.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Why were they cut?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Because of the pandemic, my boss is not generating enough money so they cut everyone's hours. I don't blame him but I am so frustrated at this whole thing.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can definitely understand your frustration. That is a big deal. Have you tried talking to him?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I want to but I don't want to be fired or annoy them.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sure that you wouldn't annoy them. It's their job to talk to their employees.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I just don't want to annoy them. Meanwhile, I am trying to get another job but no luck so far. :(

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you signed up for any job recruiting sites or consulted your state labor department office?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yep and unfortunately I still have not receive any response.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What about asking your neighbors if they need any help with child care or yard work?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No one in the neighborhood has kids and everyone does their own yard work around my area.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow. It sounds like you are really having a tough time. What do you think the next step in finding new employment is? What are your options?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: At this point, I don't know. I have thought about doing art commissions and selling them online but I don't know if my abilities are enough. Plus there is a lot of completion in the field.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That sounds like a fantastic idea, and even if there is competition, there are still a lot of people that might buy your art from Etsy or Amazon.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You know what? I think I'll try it out. :)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like you have a plan. I know that you will do great. Is there anything else that I can help you with today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think that is it. Thank you for the encouragement. :)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You're welcome! Have a nice day!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: You too!  :)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thanks!

situation: My son is a recovering meth addict and has relapsed again. After two weeks of using he admitted himself back into rehab as he as done many times in the past without a lot of success. I am afraid it will be the same this time, that he will get out of rehab and then start using again.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: HI, how are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am not doing well. I am very worried about my son's chances of recovery after so many relapses and frustrated because I know there is really nothing I can do to change things for him.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You must be very frustrated from feeling unable to do something.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am. He is a different person when he's not on drugs but when he is using he can get really mean and he scares me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I remember when my mother was having end of life mental declining issues. I understand feeling as if the person is changing and having unusual moods and mood swings.   Do you have anyone you can talk to?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I try not to think about the situation, because I don't see him since he lives in another state. But I can't get everything out of my mind. And yes I have my husband to talk to, that helps some. It's when I'm trying to sleep or when my husband isn't home that's the worst for me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are feeling the way anybody in your situation would fee like. It must be helpful to have a husband who listens and can be supportive when he is there. Have you always had sleep issues?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It does help but he's as frustrated and upset as I am. I can't sleep at night for dwelling on everything and I don't know how to stop the thoughts about all the terrible things that could happen. When I'm upset about something I cannot sleep but if I'm in a good state of mind I sleep well.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Being upset can make someones mind race and make it really difficult to rest or get any sleep at all. Have you ever gone through anything like this before?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I've been dealing with my son's addiction and recovery and relapses for the last three years.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can relate to being close to someone who is addicted. It can feel very sad and lonely at times. I would want to change the person in my life but just has to learn to let go instead. It is a process that takes years to learn.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am realizing that more every day. If it wasn't my son and just a good friend I would probably end the relationship but a mother can't do that to her own children, you know what I mean? And you are so right. I can't change or control him, I can only change and control myself and my responses to him. That is so difficult to do.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, I understand how it can feel like our children are always our babies and we want to fix everything for them. Sometimes it feels like we can change them or re raise them if we just do more for them. Have you had the chance to speak to a close friend or pastor about this situation?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not really. Most of my friends haven't dealt with that so they don't really understand what I am going through, or even what my son is going through. But you're right, I have to learn to let go and let him fix his own problems with no intereference from me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are very self aware and have good insight. I notice that you are very in touch with your feelings. You can use writing as a way to release emotions and express yourself, as you are now.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you, I will try that. Like a journal maybe?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, I can recommend a journal on Amazon that I also use. Thank you for sharing with me today. Please reach back out anytime.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you so much! I appreciate what you do!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thank you!

situation: I am sad about not having family near me. I am in a town all alone.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi! How are you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi, I am in a town all alone and far from family. I have been really depressed.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Why are you in a town all alone?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I moved away and I thought I would be happier on my own but now all I do is think about them and miss them. Nobody ever has time to talk on the phone so I am all alone.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you have the option to move back? Because family is the most important thing at the end of the day

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, I was living with my mom and dad but when I moved out my brother got my room. He and I didnt get along so he would never want me to move back into the house with them either.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: At this point, you will have to ask your family to make time to talk with you and im sure they will understand if they hear where you are coming from

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is a good idea, I can reach out to my mom. Maybe I should write her an email or something?My brother was a bully and so he is a big reason that I moved. My parents encouraged us to fight and argue, it wasn't a very fun place to live. I've always been closer to my mom so she is much more understanding. She helps me pay my monthly bills too.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah reach out to your mum, and although you fight and argue, they are still your family at the end of the day Thats nice of her :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, that is a good point. I have always heard the blood is thicker than water. I have also been upset lately because I had to leave all my pets when I moved out. I left my dog and my cat back at home.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you visited them ever since?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, I am too far away to stop by or go for the weekend. I would have to plan a whole trip and I cant really afford it yet. My brother knew that the pets were mine but he demanded that he keep them there and I didnt want to fight with him so I said fine.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If they are your pets you should be able to keep them but you did the right thing as to not argue with your brother and your parents could  help you look after them

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, you are right. Once he let the gate open just so my dog would run away, he says he didn't do it but I know him and he is really mean and vengeful. He would also pull pranks on me that were just cruel. He once told me that my cat was ran over but he was just hiding her at a friends house for a week.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah, he doesn't seem very nice, but its good that you don't retaliate negatively

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, I used to be really depressed living there. I haven't thought about why I moved but you are helping me remember that i'm better off now. I am lonely but so much happier not living in the turmoil over there.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Its a good thing you moved then because at least now you are away from him You should always try to be happy

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yep, I am feeling better today . Thank you for listening. Maybe I should just get a new rescue dog or something like that today? I don't want to dwell on my brother or that negativity. I am feeling a lot more hopeful just sharing my feelings.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes its good to not dwell on the past like that. Have a good day :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks! Have a great day too!

situation: I have trouble sleeping during the night because my son is allergic to something in the house and it keeps me up during the night because he is constantly sneezing and blowing his nose.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, how are you doing?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm fine and how are you ?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I'm doing well. Did you have anything you wanted to talk about?
 I'm doing well. Did you have anything you wanted to talk about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes I am problems with sleeping.
 *having

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Is getting a good night's sleep becoming an issue?
 Is getting a good night's sleep becoming an issue?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes very much so

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Tons of people deal with this on many levels.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I understand

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I can feel that this affects you in a negative way.
 I can feel that this affects you in a negative way.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I have had issues in the past personally with sleep. Many of my friends too.
 I have had issues in the past personally with sleep. Many of my friends too.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I just don't know what to do anymore it's even affecting my work b/c I dont get any sleep

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
There are a lot of things out there to try and get your sleep pattern in better shape.
 There are a lot of things out there to try and get your sleep pattern in better shape.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes and I have tried many

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
What about valerian root or other herbs?
 What about valerian root or other herbs?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I never thought of that I will definitely give it a try

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You could also try adjusting your diet and eating habits.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you for all of your help , this is greatly appreciated.  I never thought of using the herbs I will give this a try soon

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
You are welcome
 You are welcome

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello are you still there?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You can also try reducing caffeine intake 6 hours before bed.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh that is great I drink coffee an hour before bed on some days

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: As you know this can keep your brain stimulated and prevent you from going to sleep.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I didn't know that

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
I used to drink too much coffee and I was tired all day but could never sleep right.
 I used to drink too much coffee and I was tired all day but could never sleep right.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thanks for the info

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Anything else I can help with?
 Anything else I can help with?

situation: I fear that I will not have a job next year, at the beginning of the year.  My emotions are in survival mode and I do not want to do anything or buy anything from this fear

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi, how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi, I am doing ok, how are you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Good thank you. Why only ok? What is bothering you?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well 2020 is bothering me, like everyone else.. earlier this year my job was terminated, then for 3 months I was not bringing in income. Now that COVID is still out there, I fear that my current job will be terminated

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'm sorry to hear that. 2020 has been tough for many, so you are not alone.  I have been through a similar situation in the beginning of the year where I have lost my job. I was fearful but I kept thinking positive thoughts and it helped me get through tough times.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: True, I have been trying to think of other things I can do, like start a business But I have been working for 20 years, that I feel like I am supposed to work for someone else and not for myself.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is a great idea. The government has great support programs for new business owners like you.  I think working for yourself is great. You can set your own hours, chart your own path. It would help with your employment situation for sure.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I feel that I have not ideas.  I feel like i have been thinking about a business idea for myself and family for some time now.  I wonder how others decide on what to do Like how to get that motivation in their head to start something really new

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: My suggestion would be to think what your local neighborhood needs - maybe it is lacking a cafe or a grocery store?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I know, i really want a niche company.. Something that no one has thought of, or is not yet in my community.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, I can identify with that. Motivation is hard - but think about your future job situation and your family.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I would love to do a zero waste company or store, since that is what I am really into.  Do you think it is hard to set up a business plan?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's a great idea! Green is the new way to go! I believe in you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh Thank You!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it will take some hard work but you can do it. There are support for you from local governments. I think you should look into it.  How are you feeling now?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think I will! I actually feel motivated to do some research on this. At least is has taken my mind off possibly not having a job

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's great to hear! My suggestion is to always think positively. You can do this!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for your help.  This really helped.  I needed to be able to speak/type to someone about this.  I believe this app/program is very helpful

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are welcome. I'm wising you all the luck and success. Have a great day!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you! You also. Have a Merry Christmas and safe Holidays

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Merry Christmas and happy 2021!

situation: I live with four friends/ housemates. I like them, but at the same time we are stuck together every day 24/7. Small things add up; I am getting increasingly annoyed by their behaviors and their inconsideration for others.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: HI

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: how may i help you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Oh I am just looking to vent. The quarantine has added a lot of anxiety on my end, and I have a hard time coping with the new normal

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand how you are feeling. This pandemic got everyone stressed and their anxiety is at an all time high

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yeah I agree with the sentiment. I like my housemates/friends if we hang out once in a while, or run into each other in the common area a few times a week. Now we are all confined under the same roof all the time

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: This pandemic will not last forever and everything will be okay soon

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: If someone doesn't remember to wash dishes, or started smoking that resulted in the whole house smelling. There is only this much I can do  I can remind them; but they are likely just saying that they are sorry and continuing doing what they do  or if the wall neighbor decided to watch TV really loud very late at night and you can't sleep. I can tolerate it if it is only once in a while, but now we are together all the time

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand that maybe you should sit down with them and be firm with them and let them know how you are feeling

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yeah they are all polite and willing to acknowledge that there is something to improve on their part. I am sure there are things that I can improve on my end too. IT is just the nature of the quarantine has really upped the anxiety level.  I don't feel the option of escaping, changing the scenery a little bit or adjusting emotionally

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you are doing the right thing by staying in the house but have you just went out side and took a walk to make you feel a little better

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: it has also changed the relationship that I have with my housemates/friends. In the past, we have fun together, now it's like we are husband and wives pointing out each other's mistakes  yeah that is a good point. I agree with you.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand it is driving you crazy being around them 24/7 now maybe you should go outside and take a walk

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Do you have similar experience. I would like to know what you think

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am quarantined with one of my friends and you know we are around each other 24/7 now to that she lost her job due to the pandemic. It does drive me crazy sometimes because i want some alone time but all i can really do at this point is just go outside and take a walk or just sit outside and that helps me most the time.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will do more of that. I guess we are all making sacrifices and learning how to cope

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you know there is nothing we can really do right now until all this is over with

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I guess. not sure how long this is going to last

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I have learned to just live with it for now because i know there is not much i can do  I am sure it is not going to last much longer

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yeah I hope so

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Just keep your head up and pray about it everything will be okay and things will eventually get better.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: thank you for chatting. IT makes me feel better already

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: your most welcome

situation: Not leaving the house is making me more and more depressed.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: hi there my name is Lisa...how are you feeling today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Honestly, it's getting to me, COVID-19, not being able to leave my house safely, having a medical issue that puts me at higher risk.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i can totally relate to that, I am feeling the same way how long have you been feeling this way?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I don't know what to do with myself. A year. I haven't left my house once.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: so it sounds like you are feeling cooped up and not knowing what to do, am i right?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, it's more than that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: can you tell me more?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm in this house, and scared. Nothing is getting better for me or anyone else I know.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: may I ask where you are located?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: In Mass.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: it's normal to feel scared.  Of course it being normal doesn't make it any better... can you get out for walks?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I live in such a populous area, I am afraid to. Especially with warmer temperatures coming.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: i see.  are you saying your sidewalks are too busy to be able to social distance properly? i find getting out for walks is helpful.  i live downtown in a small city.   i wear a mask while I walk and veer onto the grass, into the bike lane or whatnot if someone comes...or I look ahead and cross the street if it's convenient...that makes me feel safer when i walk.  i have a medical condition too.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I just wish I could do that.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: what is it that is stopping you, are the sidewalks too crowded? without my walks I'd go crazy...it's my daily stress reliever....i am at home 23 hours a day are you planning to get a vaccine and if so do you know how long you have to wait? i agree with you it's a scary time...but things will get batter....the more people get vaccinnes, the better things will get for all of us are you still there?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am here. Just sad.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: so if I understand you correctly, being stuck at home for a whole year is making you feel very sad.   Do you connect with people by telephone, zoom, online, email and so on?  It's important to connect as often as you can with people you care about

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do sometimes.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: do you have a back yard or a patio you can go out on, which might be less scary than going for a walk?  Sometimes just the fresh air on my face is a relief

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I do sometimes each my lunch in the yard.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: thats good you are connecting with people, and have lunch outdoors.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks. It feels good.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: those are both important strategies for your low mood, keep it up

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks so much for talking things through with me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: you are so welcome..stay safe, it's been a pleasure listening to you

situation: lost job went to prison

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi. Hope that you have some good thoughts to share with me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Totally, I am here to help! Tell me what's the issue so we can work it through

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Cannot seem to shake the blues even though I exercise and have a friend I regularly talk with.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you think your mood is related to any current or recent events in your life?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes. To handle my previous depression I self medicated and ended up going to prison. Made my situation worse. Now trying to adjust but depression is worse having lost job and social stauts

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Feeling down after an experience like that is certainly understandable, and I suspect that might be the main cause of your depression.
 Regarding losing your job, I wouldn't worry too much. New businesses are opening each day as the pandemic slowly goes away. You will have plenty of job opportunities. Similarly, you will also regain your confidence and social status as you interact with society more.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, but its a vicious circle. Get blue and find it hard to strive. Unfortunately many jobs require background checks these days. Even Amazon! Hope with the vaccine that things will look up.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am feeling optimistic about this vaccine, you will see how it helps us all slowly get our lives back to how they were.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah. The covid thing is an additional layer of isolation and depression.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
It will soon be over hopefully
 Hey, just try to focus on improving yourself as a person. Try to find an activity you enjoy doing. Keep exercising, that's great for your body and mind. And definetely talk to any closed one you have if you need to.
 Hey, just try to focus on improving yourself as a person. Try to find an activity you enjoy doing. Keep exercising, that's great for your body and mind. And definetely talk to any closed one you have if you need to.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: political situation in US not helping like with most recent aid bill. BTW how do we know when this HIT is complete?  Yes, striving to gain 10 lbs of lean mass.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I agree, but it's not like we have any major control over that unfortunately.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Serenity prayer. Lord grant me.. to control ..

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You have to hit "quit" and fill the questionarie. And I am sure you can get really strong! Just keep working out :D

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: How about you? Holding up to Covid-19 insanity?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It has been a rough time, but I have just been focusing on work, and hopefully it will be all over

situation: I'm so scared my job will fire me when they find out about my alcohol abuse

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there, how are you doing?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: IM HANGING IN THERE HOW ARE YOU?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am okay. What would you like to talk to me about?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Well, i have a problem with drinking alcohol
I am so scared my job will find out

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Do you drink when you are at work?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have started calling in atleast twice as week. I have started drinking at work close to the end of my shift in the bathroom

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That does sound like it might be a big problem. How are  your employers reacting?  It sounds like you have a big problem that needs professional help, would you agree?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: They have not mentioned anything to me yet.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think it is likely to have been noticed though, at least.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I agree it sounds like a really big problem, but what should i do?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I think the best thing would be to be really honest with your employers and ask if you can have time off for professional help.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That is a good idea

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you employers likely to be sympathetic?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: my main concern, that makes me fearful is being fired, and being the talk of the workplace

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah people do love to idly gossip don't they? I've been through that myself. I know it's hard to ignore them but it's best to try.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My employer is an well known organization that employees over 3,000 people.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I do not know if they can fire you if you have a legitimate medical problem though. Well you could always shame them if they unfairly fired you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Very good to know that I will look into it. It did not start overnight and I don't expect it to end overnight
 lol, yes that is true. Thank you so much for your time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Large corporations are always over concerned about their images. (we have to talk for fifteen minutes :))

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
oh ok I thought it was just for 10 lines, lol
 but none the less, there are people really going through that scenario I just portrayex

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: naw there's a time thing too :). Anyway, what sort of therapy do you think would be appropriate for you?  To be honest, and I mean this sincerely, I've been through it :(

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I believe rehabilitative therapy would be best

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It can be very expensive though. In my country you can get it for free but there's a long waiting list.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have hads  a few drinks often but never abused it. thankfully.  Well here you might can get help for free, but it does have to be paid back

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Heh be grateful :)  Are you in the US? Our healthcare system is a little less rigid here I think (UK)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes Im in the United States (Alabama) 
 I heard of the UK so many times but truthfully I know nothing about it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Ah you are a long way away from me. I hope that you do manage to sort out your problems though and definitely do talk to your employers Ah it's quite nice here. We pay for healthcare out of our taxes so it's free at source.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will eventually get the courage to, I know how people talk around here, especially in the South\

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Gossipy people are the worst. Just remind yourself that they all have skeletons in their closets too

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: great advice

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Well anyway, I hope I have been able to offer your  some useful advice. I wish you all of the very best in the future.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think also the best thing to do is to talk to great people like you

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: (can you press quit first and do the survey as I am not able to do it from my side) Well thanks :)

situation: My turning another year older and still have nothing to show for my life, no friends or career. I'm undiagnosed autistic with mental health issues and I'm very alone and unsupported

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello there.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there something you want to discuss?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
yes
 My birthday's coming up and I feel a lot of self-hatred for what I've failed to accomplish by this age.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So it sounds like the birthday is a time for self-reflection, and you're not happy with where you are right now?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yea that's right. It's weighing heavy on my mind at the moment

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is this something you've felt on previous birthdays?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes, the older I get the worse the feeling is. I didn't used to hate birthdays when I was younger

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I'll confess I have felt the same way, especially with "milestone" birthdays (30, 40, 50, etc.). Do you think you are possibly expect too much in terms of achievements?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Yes, those are hard. My 30th was difficult. Society has so many wrong ideas of where people need to be, and it just doesn't apply to me. I'm so different from normal people.
 maybe, but being autistic, even average achievements are hard for me. I have to work so hard just to be average
 May I ask, how did you deal with your own birthday depression? Maybe I can learn from you

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: As I became older, i started to wonder what terms like  "normal people" and being "average" really mean. Am I comparing myself to something that even exists?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's what I've been thinking about too. It probably doesn't exist, but society would have us believe otherwise

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's so true. It's tough to deal with. But I will same, as I became older, I just stopped caring as much about what other's think. It just happened.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yep, it sure is. I figure people stop caring that much when they get older

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Also, I'm sure there are some things in life you are good at, even if those aren't what society seems to value.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I've already stopped caring what people younger than me think. They just seem so young to me
 yea, it's just hard when I struggle so much with employment. My issues really keep me from succeeding in jobs
 Sure, there are things I can do; but they don't seem to help me with anything important in life, if you know what I mean

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah, I hear you. Are you talking to others -  such as professionals, etc. - about your autism?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I was forced into therapy as a young person. I think it put me off seeking professional help because I hated the experience.
 But I talk to other autistic people in support groups online
 Also, I'm sorry to cut this off but I need to go soon. I want to tell you you're quite compassionate and kind

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Being "forced" to do anything is usually a turn off. But it's good to hear there are groups online to help.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I've enjoyed talking to you
 Yes indeed
 It's not a great experience unless you choose it

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh, thanks. It's always nice to have a real conversation with someone.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
For sure. May I ask if you're planning to become a counselor?
 I didn't know if this simulation is for people to get training for that type of wokr

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I haven't planned on it, but I am looking for a career change. But no, this wasn't training for me. Not sure what this simulation is all about :)

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Oh cool. I think this is a pretty interesting platform
 haha me too, but it seemed interesting

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yep. Oh well, hope this was worth while for you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I came here the other day and never got matched with anyone so I left.
 Thanks, it was. And you seem really cool
 I guess we can wrap up since it's over 15 mins
 and they said we can stop after 15 i think

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Thanks. Be sure to do something enjoyable on your birthday.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you:) Hope you enjoy your day/night

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: OK. Do I just hit "Quit"? I forget.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I think we both have the finish button. So we can click any time.
 I'll exit now. See you:)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Take care.

situation: I feel I am getting looked over for a promotion/raise at work and it is causing anxiety and fear that I don’t have longevity at my company and that I’m not being valued.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi there, how are you  today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I’m doing ok. Have been feeling a bit anxious about my job lately.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is this because of the pandemic? I know a lot of people are worrying about their jobs because of this.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think it’s partially due to the pandemic. I am struggling progressing at my company and have been up for a raise for a year now that still hasn’t gone through. It’s making me feel like they don’t value me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that. So, I hear you saying that you feel you are not being valued for your work?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah exactly. I want to feel like I am an important member of the team and right now I’m not feeling that and it makes me feel like I don’t have a future with this career anymore. But now is not the time for a career change.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No I guess that it isn't. Have you tried talking to your employer about this? Or even your co workers?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think that’s the part that gets me anxious. I don’t really know how to communicate this to my superiors. There isn’t a lot of open communication now that we all work from home.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Is there someone intermediate that you can talk to ? Or perhaps could you contact a union? There is help out there.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Maybe going to HR would be the smart move? They might have some resources on how to navigate relationships now that we aren’t in the office, right?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, I think that they would. Ultimately they are trained to help people who are in your situation.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have started applying to other jobs, but I don’t have a lot of hope with that right now. Maybe it is better to looks at more options at my current job. Getting up the courage to speak out can be hard though!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Would you at least get a good reference from your previous employer?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think so! I have a cordial relationship with everyone, but I know my work speaks for itself. I think I need more confidence to ask for what I know I deserve.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Perhaps you could consider some sort of counselling to help you with you self esteem? I did it and it was great.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That’s a really good idea. I think much of the anxiety I have with work stems from self esteem issues.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: There are people out there who can help. Would you consider anti anxiety medication?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think I’m much more open to trying counseling before I would venture to trying medication options. Practicing talking to people at work might really help me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes I think that it would. Just start slowly - do not dive straight in at the deep end. But you seem to have a good and sensible perspective about this.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am trying to remain level-headed about this. I know this is within my control and I just need to muster up the courage to do something about this situation that has me feeling anxious and stressed!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I wish you all of the best with it! Thank you for talking to me.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks. Appreciate the perspective.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Goodnight :)

situation: I will be losing my job in January.  I am very worried about looking for another one at this time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hope all is well

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: well, i'm losing my job in january so that is freaking me out

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I can understand your feelings but I am sure there is a way out

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I've not looked for a job in 20 years!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I was also in same situation and can fully understand how you feel but trust me you will enjoy once you start looking for job

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I've got to do a new resume and then figure out how people even apply these days

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Trust me it is easy these days to update resume as many templates available online and applying for job is easy as well. Many online options are available and you get a quick 
response.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I've tried to get in touch with the unemployment office but they do not answer phones lately.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Need not to worry about it. You can use search engine and just type the job matching your profile and apply online. Trust me you will be surprised to see many options.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well it is freaking me out - I will not be able to pay my bills if I don't have a job.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I was exactly in same situations and can fully understand but trying all your options to look for job is good - apply online, call friends or family and see if they have openings where they work, look work online if you get any temporary work which can enable you to pay bills till you find better job.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm willing to do temp work, etc.. I just have to make enough $ to pay the main bills like mortgage, etc.. it makes me so afraid that I won't be able to do t hat

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I am sure you will be able to manage all this and it is just temporary feeling you have.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: 7 i'm very upset that we are even losing our jobs.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: No need to upset as it part of life. Call your best friend and discuss your feeling and I am sure you will feel good.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: She's just been through this looking for a job thing so she should have some great answers and places to help me maybe

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Excellent!! - I am sure she will have very good suggestion and will be great support for you. It is always good to talk to people you are close.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that is true!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You can always share and contact me if you need any help. I will be happy to help. Take care.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: ok thank you

situation: I am recently divorced. My ex-husband has moved in with his new, much younger girlfriend and they are discussing marriage. Although I am happy the marriage is over, I'm depressed because I have not had any romantic offers while he has moved on with no problem.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello, is someone there?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello, my name is Jonathan. May I ask who I am speaking to today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hi Jonathan, I'm Keri.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hi Keri. How are you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm feeling a little bit down today. I've been feeling a little bit down a lot lately.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh no. I'm sorry to hear that. What do you think would be the cause of you feeling down lately?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: My ex-husband is moved in with his new girlfriend. They might get married.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh Keri, that must feel awful. Was it a recent separation between you two?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: He started dating a few months after we split up. I don't know if this is the only person he's dated but I think so. He really moved on quick.  Hi, are you still there?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yeah I understand what you're going through. I was in a similar position not too long ago. I had a hard time wrapping my head around how fast my ex could move on from what we had. How does it make you feel that he's trying to start a new relationship so soon after yours?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: It makes me feel like he didn't learn anything from our relationship or like he thinks he's better than me because he has someone new and I don't. I'm trying to take some time and be alone and be okay with being alone, which is good I think, but sometimes I feel really really lonely too. Like maybe I won't ever find anyone.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That must feel really isolating. But working on yourself and becoming a better you is the only way to move on in a healthy manner. I think you're doing the right thing in waiting and learning to love yourself before you can open up and love another.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thank you! Exactly. But it is scary. I am guessing from your name that you identify as male (please forgive me if that is incorrect), so you might not understand what it feels like to be an older woman when your ex starts dating someone a lot younger. Men only get better and more desirable with age, not so for women.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I might not understand that particular aspect, but self worth is still self worth no matter the context. I'd say try not to pay any mind to what he's up to. The grass always looks greener on the other side of the fence, but if you just focus on watering your grass (making yourself happy) then you'll have a much more fulfilling life.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Haha, watering my own grass. That is a good way to put it. Thank you for talking to me for a few minutes. It makes me feel a little bit better to have someone agree that it is okay to spend time working on me, instead of thinking that it is sad or pathetic or an excuse. I really appreciate it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Oh absolutely Keri. Thanks for opening up and sharing your experience and feelings with me for just a few minutes. It was great to talk to you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Have a good night, Jonathan. :-)

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Happy Holidays Keri. Keep your head up!

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Happy holidays.

situation: I am losing my job in January.  I am very worried about looking for work right now

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not so great - found out i'm losing my job in january

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: On no I am sorry to hear that! I know that must be tough.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes I'm not even sure what steps to take right now.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yea that can be difficult. Have you started looking for something else?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not yet, I'm not even sure where to begin.  I have not looked for a job in 20 years.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You could try looking online there are a few websites that you could put a resume on and maybe find something that way

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I've tried calling the unemployment office to get some ideas but they do not answer their phones.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Or you could look for some side jobs like cleaning or organizing things until you find something more permanent.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yeah, i'm kinda old for that - LOL!!!  My back would not hold up to that - and my salary wouldn't be comparable.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yea it seems to be hard to get up with a lot of places at this time. Persistent is the key.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Well I hear that their  offices are closed and you have to do everything online - so not sure if they can even help me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes i understand right now is a very bad time to find anything with so many places closed.  Do you have any family or friends to reach out to for help if needed?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think I might be able to call my friend, but she's in another state

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you gave any thought to babysitting or are there any crafts you are good at making that maybe you could try to sell?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That would not be enough money.  I would not be able to pay my bills.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand your concern there but it could help pay some as to help you not to get to far behind until you find something.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that's true but i don't want to lose my house either

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: If i were you i would call your friend maybe she has some suggestions of things you could do based off of your skills.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: she may be able to help - she could at least send me in the right direction of where to look and maybe how to freshen up my resume.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yea and if not it wont have hurt to talk to her about it and all of the worry off your mind.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: that is true - i just hate to bother her, she's got so much on her plate already.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It does not hurt to try. Maybe call her and tell her you have some things on your mind and could use some advice from her if it is not to much of a bother.

situation: I'm disgusted that the professors don't see me as a human with real emotions and feelings.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: How can I help you today?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm disgusted with my professors that they don't see me as a human with real emotions and feelings.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand that you are feeling like your professors don't see you as a person with feelings. What happened to make you feel this way?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have testing all the time and study non-stop, but the professors don't seem to encourage or support me.  They just see me as a number I feel.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That's hard to feel like they are treating you like a number when you are working so hard.  Have you talked to them about it?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
It's hard to trust them when I'm seen as 1 of 100 people.  If I am struggling they don't flinch or ask me how they can assist.  I just am expected to 
 tough it out.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I had a similar experience in my college days. It was very difficult to have so much pressure to succeed and feel so alone.  You can do though because you are working hard and want to succeed.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Thanks for the encouragement.  Where do I start to help myself feel better?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I see you are anxious about this when talking to you, but have you thought about getting some support from other students in your position?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That sounds like a good idea.  Maybe they are feeling this way too.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Also sometimes just stepping back and taking some deep breaths can help to alleviate some anxieties. Have you tried that ever?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I will have to give that a try.  I've done that in the past and had forgotten just how much that helped me before.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: That is reassuring that it worked before for you and  can work again.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I'm so worried about failing because I just don't feel the support from my professors to care either way for my successes.  It's so different from high school and home when my parents encouraged me and supported me full-heartedly.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: College can be quite a change from the protective atmosphere of home.  Have you tried any support groups or religious groups for encouragement?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am religious so perhaps I could seek a group out and they could help with encouraging me.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I was a  part of a religious group in college and they were very supportive even praying for me on exam days.  I felt they helped me with my success by planting seeds of positive feedback to me.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I used to be part of one during high school years, and that sounds like it would help.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you starting to feel better about the situation?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Yes, I do thinking about other people filling in the gaps and supporting me when others won't or can't. Thanks for chatting.  This was helpful.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: You are welcome.  I wish you well.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I think I have a few tools now to make things better.

situation: I have lost my job, and my entire industry has been shut down due to Covid-19, and I am struggling to find work that is safe for me to do from home.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hey, how are you doing?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
Not the best, but I'm surviving.
 hello?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
By surviving, it is more of a personal situation
 or an outside dilemma affecting you. is it*

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I just haven't been able to find any work. I guess that is an outside dilemma, but being this behind on bills and feeling this helpless to change my situation has negatively impacted my mood.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I see what you mean. Finding work in this environment can be stressful as well. In any case, I can say that a good way to start is to account for all transactions you make.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have already been budgeting extensively, the issue is I can't make enough to cover my basic expenses, no matter how much I cut them

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Are you also pressed for time? Time management can be a predicament as well.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I have lots of free time, just waiting for unemployment to respond to my claims, applying for jobs online, and trying services like mturk to earn what I can from home in the meantime.  I spend a good portion of my day trying to apply for everything I can and fixating on mturk tasks to make me feel like I'm accomplishing something

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Wow, it's slightly uncanny the way our situations are similar. For someone who is also not in the best place financially and just making the bare minimum, it can feel hopeless sometimes. I know you are feeling stressed at the moment, but I've realized focusing on the long term helps ease the pressure of current stressors.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I get to a point in my day where it feels like I've done everything I can for that day, and then I just have to try not to get too stir crazy.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I feel that if you can focus on using your extra time as an investment into yourself (whether by reading, picking up a hobby, or working out), you can feel more accomplished and at ease with what you are doing in life.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I've been trying to look ahead, but this year has already set me back so much from my intended career path that it's frustrating. I just want my life back.
 That is good advice. I have been doing a lot more art during this time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Being progressive is difficult, but is one of the things in life which compound very easily. I believe many aspects in life are connected and that improving in some parts improves you as a whole

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Some days it's tempting to just do nothing and wait for it all to be over, but I feel better when I accomplish something for the day

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: And if these "improvements" can also make you money/increase your worth/ease your mind, then that can be called a success. In my opinion atleast.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I wish someone was interested in buying my art! That would be a huge confidence boost

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content:
Yeah, but imagine how it would if you had days like that knowing it isn't a bad thing to relax. You have already put in the work in being comfortable before, allowing to enjoy your time however way you wanted.
 Looks like you already have an interest which can help you in many ways in life. That's really good.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: yes, I miss being able to actually relax. When you are financially behind it feels like everything is just making it worse. thank you for chatting with me and helping me earn the bonus for this task!

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Yes, the tension and pressure only seems to mount on, but there will always be things like that in life. It is important to remember whether such problems are in your control or not. If they are, you know for sure that you can change how you are presently. No problem, this is a very interesting experience for me too.

situation: I feel hopeless. Sometimes I just want to end my life. It isn't worth it.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Hello. What are some of the quickest ways to kill myself?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you talked to someone about this?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: No, why bother? Everyone is just cruel. People are selfish.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I understand people can be cruel and selfish, but not everyone is. Some people want to help! Life is definitely worth living for.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Not at all. Why would I want to live in a degenerate world where millions die everyday? Be it hunger, disease, murder... Human beings are disgusting. We should go extinct.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Talking to someone you are close to, like a family member who cares about you, can definitely help. Can you try talking to someone?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Family? My family kicked me out of their own house 3 years ago. I am nothing but a burden for them. They would just appreciate if I died.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: What about close friends, or people you care about? I am sure they would not want to lose you.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I have NO ONE that cares about me. Not a single being.
 Are overdoses painful?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I care

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I doubt you do. You are just getting paid to be here.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Just from chatting with you, I can feel how sad you are about life and maybe a little overwhelmed with what the next step of life is.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What would be that next step if I asked you?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: I used to be really sad about life too. However, helping others who are sad helped me become happier, knowing that I am helping others also become happier.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: What makes you think someone as useless as me could provide any help?

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Take a deep breathe and think about any happy memories. If you are sad right now, the only way is up from here! I believe no one is useless. We were all brought on this earth for a reason.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: That's a very optimistic view...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Like I said, I used to be sad too and not optimistic. But now I am optimistic because I know what it is like to be sad, and I don't want others to feel how I have felt in the past.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I guess that's a way to view it...

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Have you tried to express your feelings in one way or another? If you don't want to talk to others, there is always other ways - such as writing, music, etc. Have you tried any different ways?  Or would you be open to trying any of those different options?

situation: I have been recently laid out because of COVID-19 and not solved yet.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Hello.

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: I am looking for another job preferably in my locality and not getting yet, since last 2 months. I will find it difficult to sustain if this continues like this for other 3 months.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: So it sounds like you've been struggling with finding a new job. It's never easy looking for a new job during these times. Why do you think you're having trouble?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Getting a job is not that difficult. But I try to find a job in my locality to avoid distant from family and having food issues.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like you're motivated and dealing with a lot. Is there a specific job that you had in mind or are you applying to different kinds of jobs?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content: Any kind of job, probably managerial. But to be in my locality since to look after my aged mom also.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: It sounds like you really care to want to be close to home. Have you talked with someone locally about jobs that would allow you to stay close to home?

dialog.speaker: seeker
dialog.content:
I have been talking to some people who my friends knows also. They show interest but cannot help me on the issue.
 Surely they will refer to some people. But it will take some time.

dialog.speaker: supporter
dialog.content: Stay motivated. If one opportunity doesn't get your desired response you may be even closer to finding the right job for you.
